There is a lot of unrest in the city
There is a lot of unrest in the city
There is some unrest in the city
There is some unrest in the city
There are stirrings of unrest in the city
There is no unrest in the city
Plague is very likely, due to the city's appalling hygiene.
City hygiene is terrible, and people can barely function. Plague is likely.
City hygiene is bad, and there very well be a plague. People don't feel well at all.
City hygiene is poor, and plague could strike. People long to feel well again.
City hygiene is not good. People expect to fall ill at any moment.
City hygiene is ok. People aren't feeling too well, but no one is very sick either.
City hygiene is good. On the whole, people are feeling well.
City hygiene is very good. The common cold is the only threat.
City hygiene is excellent. The only illnesses are the result of too much wine.
City hygiene is almost perfect. An occasional stubbed toe is the only concern.
City hygiene is perfect. People couldn't be healthier!
Too few households have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Only a few households have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Too many households don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Many households don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Most households don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
More households need access to a healer from an Infirmary
Several households still don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Some households still don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Only a very few households don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Only a few households don't have access to a healer from an Infirmary
All of your citizens have access to a healer from an Infirmary
Too few households were able to eat last month
Few households were able to eat last month
Far too many households weren't able to eat last month
Many households weren't able to eat last month
Most households weren't able to eat last month
Many households don't have a reliable source of food
Several households still don't have a reliable source of food
Some households still don't have a reliable source of food
Few households missed meals last month
Very few households weren't able to eat last month
All of your citizens ate well last month

Maintenance Office keeps the city's structures sound.

Watchpost protects good citizens from criminals.
Current food production and importation can support
Approximate loads of food per month
The city currently has no sources of food.
Your city's wise philosophers have a good chance of winning the games. Don't change a thing!
With a little luck, you might win the games. Build a few more Colleges and Podiums to increase your chances.
If the gods are with you, you might win the games. Build more Colleges and Podiums to improve your chances.
Your philosophers are not likely to win the games. They need many more Colleges and Podiums to perfect their theories.
Your city has little chance of doing well at the games because it doesn't have nearly enough philosophy buildings.
Your strong contestants are sure to excel at the next games. They have all the facilities that they need.
Your contestants have a good shot at winning the games. Build more athletic facilities to improve their odds.
Your city's contestants are average at best. Increase their chances of winning by building more Gymnasiums.
Your city won't have much of a chance of winning the games unless it devotes more resources to athletics.
You'll need a lot more athletic facilities if you want your contestants to have a good showing at the games.
Your actors are very well-trained, and their chances of winning the games are excellent.
Your actors are good overall, but they would perform better at the games if you built a few more drama facilities.
Your actors need to hone their crafts more if they are to do well at the games. Build more drama facilities.
The judges at the games are likely to roll their eyes at your actors. Build a lot more drama facilities.
Your actors aren't much of a presence. Build many more drama facilities if you want them to do well at the games.
Local contestants have an excellent chance of winning the games and bringing honor on our city.
Our contestants might be able to win the games. Increase their chances by building more cultural facilities.
Our contestants might not embarrass us at the games. Build more cultural facilities to help them do well.
The prognosis for your success in the games is not good. Your city is lacking some cultural pursuits.
You have only a snowball's chance in Hades of winning the games. Devote more resources to culture.
You are hosting the Games in
You are hosting the Games now.

Meager wages are forcing people to find greener pastures elsewhere.
Tired of not getting paid, people are leaving because of the city's prolonged debt.
People are leaving the city because of excessive military service.
People don't want to be ruled by such an unpopular figure and are leaving the city.
The city's history of charging high taxes is causing people to leave.
Your penchant for underpaying people in the past is causing people to emigrate.
People are leaving the city because of past prolonged debt.
People are leaving the city because of past military service.

This building is trying to find workers.
WARNING: This building needs access to a road
This building cannot access the labor pool.
WARNING: This Building is on fire!
Nothing functions properly while it's cursed!
This building lacks the raw materials it needs
This building is missing many workers.
This building is missing a few workers.
This building has all the workers it needs.
This is the road running through an Agora
You may be able to harvest timber from this wooded area by building a Timber Mill, or you can clear the land if you choose.
This barren rock holds no value. You cannot build anything upon it, and people cannot walk over it.
Water is Poseidon's domain. You might be able to find sea urchins or fish here.

Description of trees - terrain features (Impassable terrain that may be cleared to allow city expansion. Timber Mills need to be near trees in order to harvest timber. (To select a building, right-click on its foundation).
This land has been left cracked and broken by an earthquake. You can bridge a straight crack by placing a road across it.
This Quarry is rich in white marble. Build a Masonry Shop to harvest the raw materials that lie here.
This open land is just waiting to be built upon.
The Water Crossing allows walkers to cross bodies of water.
Description of road connecting city to king road.
The copper ore in this rock can be smelted into bronze at a Foundry. Bronze can be made into armor and sculpture.
The silver ore in this rock can be converted into drachmas at a Mint.
This is Special rock. (this text should not appear anywhere)
Nothing can be built on the beach, but people can walk across it.
The tide is in, and the beach is submerged.
People can make their way across marshes, but nothing can be built on this muddy terrain.
This fertile land can play host to farms, olive trees, grapevines, orange trees and livestock.
The urchins here are waiting in their beds for an urchin gatherer to collect them.
Fish are plentiful here! Build a Fishery to haul them out of the water.
This hot, molten lava will burn anything in its path. Watch out!
This cold rock was once molten lava. Nothing can be built upon it.
This Quarry has a bountiful supply of black marble. Build a Black Marble Workshop to harvest it.
This is mysterious orichalc ore. Once it is transformed into orichalc metal at a Refinery, it can be used to adorn buildings.
Parks are pleasant spots for people to unwind after a hard day's work.
Healers from the Infirmary cure what ails the city's residents.
With a full staff, this Infirmary is sending out healers as quickly as it can.
This Infirmary is slightly understaffed. It is still sending out healers, but people are going a little longer between check-ups.
This Infirmary only has half of the workers it needs. People are putting up with their aches and pains until a healer finally visits them.
This Infirmary hardly has any workers at all. Citizens' hygiene practices could worsen!
With only one employee, this Infirmary can barely function.
This Infirmary has no employees. Citizens are applying dangerous home remedies to cure themselves.
This building is on fire! The healers are a little too preoccupied to make their rounds.
A god has cursed this building, and healers will not provide cures until the curse is lifted.
The College teaches philosophers to ponder the world around them.
With full employment, this College is unleashing new philosophers on the city as fast as it can.
This College is slightly understaffed. It's still training philosophers, but at a slower pace.
This College has around half of the workers it needs. With so few people on staff, it's taking a lot of time to teach new philosophers.
This College has far too few employees. The teaching of new philosophers has slowed to a crawl.
This College has only one lonely employee. He is doing his best to teach new philosophers, but it is slow going.
This College has no employees. The halls are silent, and no philosophers are learning anything here.
This building is on fire, and the philosophers here have concluded that burns are no fun!
This College has been cursed and cannot produce philosophers until the curse has been lifted. Dunce caps are in vogue here.
At the Drama School, would-be actors learn to emote, emote, emote.
This Drama School has all the workers it needs to turn ordinary men into drama powerhouses.
If you want high-class actors in this city, this Drama School is going to need more employees. It's still turning out actors, but at a slower rate.
This Drama School has around half the employees it needs and is training new actors very slowly indeed.
With just a handful of workers, this Drama School is taking quite a long time to train new actors.
With only one employee, this Drama School cannot teach new actors very quickly at all.
This Drama School has no employees. No actors are learning to traipse across the stage here.
Fire! Actors are running for their lives!
This building has been cursed! The teachers here cannot even instruct in the ancient art of mime!
At the Gymnasium, scrawny youths become muscle-bound giants.
This Gymnasium has all the workers it needs to produce athletes and world-class competitors.
This Gymnasium is lacking a few workers. It's taking a little longer than expected for the youths to develop strong muscles.
This Gymnasium has around half the workers it needs. Spaghetti-armed youths are the order of the day.
With only a few staff members, the youths training here are still weaklings. It's taking a very long time for any of them to attain athlete or competitor status.
The skeleton staff that works at this Gymnasium can only do so much. The Gymnasium is turning out new athletes or competitors at an excruciatingly slow rate.
With no employees, this Gymnasium is dormant, and no competitors or athletes are being trained here.
Fire! The athletes here have quickly dashed away!
This Gymnasium is cursed! Well-toned muscles have been replaced by flab, and the surrounding neighborhood is not entertained by watching the athletes just sit around!
This Gymnasium is sanctified! It will produce twice the number of athletes until the sanctification wears off.
Competitions of strength and speed take place here. If your city is lucky, it might even be able to host Games in this Stadium one day.
This Stadium is not operational. It has employees, but it needs competitors from a Gymnasium to host contests.
This Stadium is the site of many a great battle of competitor against competitor.
This Stadium has no employees and cannot host any competitions.
Right now, there are no competitions here.
The Stadium is on fire! Run!
This cursed Stadium cannot host competitions!
This blessed Stadium provides more entertainment than usual.
Your city is proud to be hosting the Games this year.
Philosophers ponder, pontificate and proselytize here.
This Podium has employees but no philosophers to spread knowledge. Make sure there are Colleges in the city to supply philosophers.
Without employees, this Podium is just a raised platform.
Right now, no philosophers are giving lectures here.
Lecture: Socrates has some questions for you
Lecture: Utopia in a Few Easy Lessons
This Podium is ablaze! Its philosophers are off looking for a cooler place to preach and are not bringing knowledge to your citizens.
This Podium is cursed! Its philosophers have become fascinated by their own navels and are not spreading their theories to the masses.
This Podium is sanctified! Double the normal number of philosophers are teaching lofty ideas to the neighborhood's residents.
Here, great tragedies and classic comedies are performed to the delight of the crowds.
This Theater has employees but needs actors to perform the shows.
This Theater is currently entertaining the masses.
This Theater has no employees, so the actors are refusing to work.
This Theater is not showing any plays right now.
Fire in the Theater! The actors here have decreed that the show will not go on until the fire is out! With no shows, the Theater cannot provide entertainment to the neighborhood.
This Theater is cursed! The actors cannot remember their lines and are not entertaining the public.
This Theater is sanctified! Twice as many actors as usual are bemusing area residents.
Here, horses are raised to serve in battle and race at the Hippodrome.
This Horse Ranch has no employees and is not raising any horses.
With a full staff, the Horse Ranch is able to care for the maximum number of horses.
This Horse Ranch lacks some workers and cannot care for a full ranch of horses.
This Horse Ranch is understaffed and can only care for a few horses
With only a few ranch hands, this Horse Ranch can barely take care of any horses.
This Horse Ranch has more horses than ranch hands. It will take a long time before any of the horses are ready to be sold.
This Horse Ranch needs a supply of wheat from a Storehouse or Wheat Farm before it can raise any horses.
This monument is a proud testament to your city's growth.
You won a grand and noble victory, and this monument is a reminder of your achievement.
Your borders have expanded, and this monument will remind you of the colony you founded.
The skill of your victorious athletes and competitors matches the grandeur of this monument.
This monument serves as a reminder of that glorious day when another city fell to your power.
The city's golden yesteryears will never be forgotten, thanks to this monument.
This monument serves as a reminder of a great hero who attained a great feat while in the service of this city.
Through your adept skill, a fellow leader agreed to become your ally. This monument serves as a reminder to your suave tongue.
Your philosophers or actors thrilled the audiences at the games. This monument is a symbol of their intellectual prowess.
The great god Zeus presented you with this monument as a token of his appreciation for retrieving the Golden Fleece.
Poseidon gladly awarded you this monument when you retrieved the Palladium.
In gratitude for retrieving the Cattle of Geryon, Demeter awarded you this monument.
Apollo, thankful that you rescued his friend Theseus from the Underworld, awarded you this monument.
Artemis, delighted with the Girdle of Hippolyte that you retrieved for her, gave you this monument.
The stalwart Ares, God of War, gave you this monument to reward you for retrieving the Mares of Diomedes.
Aphrodite was overjoyed with the Golden Apples of the Hesperides and awarded you this monument.
The fleet-footed Hermes gave you this monument when you found his Winged Sandals.
The wise Athena presented you with this monument when you found her Mirror Shield.
When you retrieved the Bow and Arrows of Hercules, Hephaestus gave this monument to you.
After you delivered the Horn of Amalthea to Dionysus, he rewarded you with this monument.
Hades gave you this monument to award you for finding the Helmet of Invisibility.
The great god Zeus presented you with this grand monument as a token of his appreciation for retrieving the Golden Fleece.
Poseidon gladly awarded you this grand monument when you retrieved the Palladium.
In gratitude for retrieving the Cattle of Geryon, Demeter awarded you this grand monument.
Apollo, thankful that you rescued his friend Theseus from the Underworld, awarded you this grand monument.
Artemis, delighted with the Girdle of Hippolyte that you retrieved for her, gave you this grand monument.
The stalwart Ares, God of War, gave you this grand monument to reward you for retrieving the Mares of Diomedes.
Aphrodite was overjoyed with the Golden Apples of the Hesperides and awarded you this grand monument.
The fleet-footed Hermes gave you this grand monument when you found his Winged Sandals.
The wise Athena presented you with this grand monument when you found her Mirror Shield.
When you retrieved the Bow and Arrows of Hercules, Hephaestus gave this grand monument to you.
After you delivered the Horn of Amalthea to Dionysus, he rewarded you with this grand monument.
Hades gave you this grand monument to award you for finding the Helmet of Invisibility.
In her beneficence, Hera rewarded you with this grand monument for sending a hero on a Journey to Olympus.
The mighty Atlas awarded you after the great hero Widened the Strait, easing the passage between the Mediterranean Sea and the Atlantic Ocean.
The goddess Hera rewarded you with this grand monument to commemorate a hero's journey to Olympus, and the hero's subsequent fall.
The strong Atlas presented this grand monument to you after you sent a mighty hero to Widen the Strait between the Mediterranean Sea and the Atlantic Ocean.
The all-powerful Zeus awarded you this monument so that people will always remember Zeus' kindness in helping to dispatch the enemy.
Poseidon, master of the ocean, gave this monument to you in thanks for finding the Cornerstone to his Sanctuary.
The gentle goddess Demeter awarded you this monument in gratitude for harvesting the Argent Maize.
The sun-kissed god Apollo presented you with this monument to honor your skill in retrieving the Tablet of the Pact.
The huntress Artemis gave this monument to you to thank you for your help in gathering animals for the menagerie.
The warlike Ares presented you with this token for your help in retrieving the missing piece of the great weapon.
Overjoyed with the Spring of Youth, Aphrodite excitedly presented you with this beautiful monument.
The god of thieves, Hermes, presented you with this monument so that all may remember the time when a great hero stole some orichalc, weakening the defenses of your foe.
The wise goddess Athena donated this monument to the city in honor of the great hero who retrieved the Golden Bridle.
So that all may remember the glorious time when a crafty hero sabotaged a fearsome weapon, Hephaestus granted this monument to the city.
Dionysus gleefully rewarded you with this monument, as thanks for helping to exploit the drunken chaos of your enemy and avenge the deaths of Dionysus' people, the Centaurs.
To mark the occasion when a brave hero ventured to Olympus and stole some Ambrosia, Hades commissioned this fine monument.
The all-powerful Zeus awarded you this grand monument so that people will always remember Zeus' kindness in helping to dispatch the enemy.
Poseidon, master of the ocean, gave this grand monument to you in thanks for finding the Cornerstone to his Sanctuary.
The gentle goddess Demeter awarded you this grand monument in gratitude for harvesting the Argent Maize.
The sun-kissed god Apollo presented you with this grand monument to honor your skill in retrieving the Tablet of the Pact.
The huntress Artemis gave this grand monument to you to thank you for your help in gathering animals for the menagerie.
The warlike Ares presented you with this grand monument for your help in retrieving the missing piece of the great weapon.
Overjoyed with the Spring of Youth, Aphrodite excitedly presented you with this beautiful, grand monument.
The god of thieves, Hermes, presented you with this grand monument so that all may remember the time when a great hero stole some orichalc, weakening the defenses of your foe.
The wise goddess Athena donated this grand monument to the city in honor of the great hero who retrieved the Golden Bridle.
So that all may remember the glorious time when a crafty hero sabotaged a fearsome weapon, Hephaestus granted this grand monument to the city.
Dionysus gleefully rewarded you with this grand monument, as thanks for helping to exploit the drunken chaos of your enemy and avenge the deaths of Dionysus' people, the Centaurs.
To mark the occasion when a brave hero ventured to Olympus and stole some Ambrosia, Hades commissioned this fine, grand monument.
Hera gave this grand monument to your city in remembrance of the time that you sent a hero to help Hera distract Zeus.
Atlas granted you this grand monument in remembrance of the happy time when you sent a strong hero to relieve Atlas of his burden.
Hera gave this monument to your city in remembrance of the time that you sent a hero to help Hera distract Zeus.
Atlas granted you this monument in remembrance of the happy time when you sent a strong hero to relieve Atlas of his burden.

This ally leader's opinion of you is very high. As long as he has the supplies to spare, he will grant your requests for food or goods. He will also send troops to defend your city if you need them and might also strike another city at your behest. If he hasn't already, he will also offer you money if you have run into debt. If this leader continues to feel this way towards you, he might one day give you a gift! Consult this ally's history to find out why his opinion of you is so high.

This ally leader has a high opinion of you. He will be willing to fulfill your requests for food and goods, provided that he can spare the items you've requested. If he hasn't already, he will also offer you money if you have run into debt. Consult this city's history for more information.

This ally leader's opinion of you is fair. They will be willing to fulfill your requests for food and perhaps even goods, provided they can spare the items you've requested. They might also be willing to offer you funds if they notice that you are in debt. For more information on this ally's opinion, consult the city history.
This ally leader's opinion of you is low. You cannot request any aid from them. Check this city's history to find out why they feel this way towards you.

This ally leader's opinion of you is very low. You cannot request anything from them, and any friendly feelings they may have had towards you are gone. In fact, if they continue to feel this way towards you, they might suspend trade. Consult this city's history to find out why they feel this way.

This ally leader has thought very highly of you for a long time! If they haven't already presented you with a gift, they will likely do so soon as long as their opinion of you doesn't worsen. To learn how your relationship with this ally has reached this high point, consult the city's history.

This ally leader has thought highly of you for some time. If you continue to improve your relationship, this city might consider giving you a gift! Consult the history of your relationship with this ally to see why they feel so warmly towards you.

This ally leader has been angry with you for awhile. If your relationship continues to worsen, this city might even sever trade relations with you. Consult this city's history to find out how things got to this low point.

This ally leader has been disgusted with you for a long time. If they haven't already suspended trade relations with you, they are likely to do so if you don't improve this relationship soon. Consult this city's history to find out why he feels this way.
This rival thinks very highly of you. He'll probably be willing to fulfill your requests for food, and he might even fulfill a request for goods. For more information, consult this rival's history.

This rival thinks highly of you. He won't entertain any of your requests, but you can always demand the things you want from him. If he feels that your military is stronger than his, he'll probably comply. Consult this rival's history for more information about your relationship.
This rival feels lukewarmly towards you. You can demand things from him, and he'll comply if your military is stronger than his. Consult this city's history for more information.
This rival doesn't like you much right now. He'll comply with your demands if your military is stronger than his is. Consult this city's history to find out why this rival feels this way.
This rival doesn't like you at all right now. If he continues to feel this way, he might one day invade your city. To find out why this rival feels this way, consult the city's history.
This rival has thought highly of you for a long time! He might even comply with a request for food or goods! Consult this city's history to find out why he feels this way.

This rival has liked you for some time. If conditions between you continue to improve, he might even willingly comply with a request for food or goods! To find out how things got this way, consult this city's history.
This rival has hated you for quite a while. If the relationship between you worsens, he might decide to invade you.
This rival has hated you for a long time. Don't be surprised if this rival invades you. To find out how things got this way, consult this city's history.

This vassal's leader thinks very highly of you. If he can, this leader will fulfill any request you have, including a request for an offensive strike. If he continues to feel this way about you, he might present you with a gift. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels so warmly towards you.

This vassal's leader thinks highly of you. He'll do what he can to fulfill any request you might have, including a request for an offensive strike. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

This vassal's leader feels lukewarmly towards you. He'll do what he can to fulfill most of your requests, though he may not entertain requests for offensive strikes. To see why this leader feels this way, consult this city's history.

This vassal's leader doesn't like you very much right now. He'll fulfill requests for goods and food, but won't risk any of his own soldiers for you for any reason. To find out why this leader feels this way, consult this city's history.

This vassal's leader doesn't like you at all right now. He won't entertain any of your requests, and if he continues to feel this way, he might even rebel! To find out why he feels this way, consult this city's history.
This vassal's leader has thought highly of you for a long time. If he hasn't already, he might present you with a gift! Consult this city's history for a record of this wonderful relationship.

This vassal's leader has thought highly of you for some time. If your relationship continues to improve, the leader might offer you a gift! Consult this city's history to find out why this vassal feels this way.
This vassal's leader hasn't liked you for a long time. If he hasn't already, he might suspend tribute payments to you. To find out why things are so rocky between you, consult the city history.

This vassal's leader absolutely despises you. He has probably already ceased trading with you, and is even now planning a rebellion. Consult this city's history to find out why your relationship has deteriorated.

This colony's leader thinks very highly of you. He'll willingly comply with any of your requests, provided he has the resources to do so. If he continues to feel this way, he might even give you a gift! Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.
This colony's leader thinks highly of you. If he can, he'll comply with any of your requests. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

This colony's leader feels lukewarmly towards you. He'll respond to most of your requests, though he might not entertain requests for offensive strikes. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

This colony's leader doesn't like you much right now. He won't risk any of his own soldiers for you, not even for city defense. He'll still comply with requests for food and goods. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way feels this way.

This colony's leader doesn't like you at all right now. He won't entertain any of your requests, and he might even rebel against you if he continues to feel this way. To find out why this leader feels this way, consult this city's history.

This colony's leader has thought very highly of you a long time. If he hasn't already, he'll probably present you with a gift! To find out how your relationship has reached this point, consult the city's history.

This colony's leader has thought highly of you for awhile. If your relationship continues to improve, he might offer you a gift! To find out why this leader feels this way, consult this city's history.
This colony's leader hasn't liked you for awhile. If he hasn't already, he'll likely stop paying you tribute. Consult this city's history to find out how things got this way.

This colony's leader has despised you for a long time. He's probably already suspended tribute payments, and rumor has it that he's planning a rebellion! Consult this city's history to find out how your relationship reached this low point.

The leader of your parent city thinks very highly of you. He'll respond to all of your requests provided he has the resources to do so. If he continues to feel this way, he might even present you with a gift! If he hasn't already, he'll also grant you money if you go into debt. To find out why this leader feels this way, consult this city's history.

The leader of your parent city thinks highly of you. He'll respond to any of your requests. If he hasn't already, he'll also grant you money if you go into debt. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

The leader of your parent city feels lukewarmly towards you. He'll respond to most of your requests, though he might not listen to a request for an offensive strike. If he hasn't already, he also might grant you money if you go into debt. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

The leader of your parent city doesn't like you very much right now. He won't grant you military forces for any reason. He'll begrudgingly grant your requests for food and, if you're lucky, for goods. Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

The leader of your parent city doesn't like you at all right now. He won't entertain any request from you. If he continues to feel this way, he might suspend trade relations with you. To find out why he feels this way, consult the city's history.

The leader of your parent city has thought very highly of you for a long time. If he hasn't already, this leader will present you with a gift! To find out why this leader feels this way, consult this city's history.

The leader of your parent city has thought highly of you for awhile. If he continues to feel this way, he might present you with a gift! Consult this city's history to find out why this leader feels this way.

The leader of your parent city hasn't liked you for awhile. If conditions continue to worsen between you, this leader might sever trade relations. Check this city's history to find out what made your relationship sour.

The leader of your parent city absolutely despises you. If he hasn't already, he will cut off all trade relations with you. Consult this city's history to find out why things are so bitter between you.
This Agora needs more employees before it can function properly.
No one will work at an Agora that has no Vendor Stalls!
This Agora has everything it needs to supply the surrounding neighborhood.
This Agora has employees but needs supplies to distribute to the neighborhood.
The peddler is waiting here for new supplies to arrive.
The peddler is out and about, supplying houses with his wares.
The peddler is on his way back to pick up more supplies.
Fire! The vendors have run away!
This Agora is cursed! No one wants its goods!
This Agora is sanctified! A hidden trove of goods has been found!
Heroes like to hang out here between exploits.
Production stopped - no boats needed in city.
Boat ordered but no wood stored.
Boats need repair, but no wood stored.
Ships from far and near sail to the Pier to exchange goods.
A boat has arrived to unload goods, but there are no longshoremen there to unload the supplies. What a waste!
This Pier lacks longshoremen, and it's taking them a long time to unload the supplies from the docked ship. Wait until the union hears about this!
This Pier is missing a longshoreman or two. The ship docked here will have to wait a little longer for all its supplies to be off-loaded.
The Pier has all the longshoremen it needs, and they are unloading supplies like there's no tomorrow.
This Pier has no employees. Hopefully, it will find some before a ship comes in to trade goods.
This Pier needs several more longshoremen. If a ship comes in, it will take some time for the workers to unload it.
This Pier lacks a few longshoremen. If a trade ship arrives, they'll take a little longer than usual to unload it.
A full staff of longshoremen is here, waiting with eager anticipation for the next trade ship to arrive.
The Fishery sends out fishing boats to catch tasty fish.
The fishermen are busy building a seaworthy boat. Once they're finished with the boat, they'll sail the high seas in search of fish.
There is no place to dock a boat here.
The Fishermen have set sail and are headed to the fishing grounds, waiting with baited breath to catch some fish.
The fishing boat is at the fishing grounds, and the fishermen are busy hauling in the catch.
The Fishermen are making sure that they have everything they need before setting sail for the fishing grounds.
The fishing boat is laden with yummy fish and is sailing back to the Fishery.
The fishermen would love to go catch some fish, but there are no fishing grounds nearby.
You ordered a halt to fishing.
Roasted fish, anyone? This Fishery is on fire and won't operate unless the fire is put out.
This Fishery is cursed! Try as they might, the fishermen here cannot catch any fish, and they won't until the curse is lifted.
This Fishery is sanctified! Fish are willingly jumping into the boat, and the fishermen expect to haul in four baskets of fish instead of the usual one!
The Palace is the heart of any city. Without it, you cannot collect taxes nor can you muster military forces.
Fire! The Palace won't function properly until the fire is extinguished!
The Palace is cursed! Nothing good will come of it!
Superintendents from the Maintenance Office make sure surrounding buildings are in good repair.
The superintendent is just checking to make sure that he has the tools he needs before he goes patrolling.
This Maintenance Office has a full staff of superintendents, ready to keep the city's buildings in tip-top shape.
This Maintenance Office doesn't have quite enough superintendents. They are doing the best they can, but some buildings in the area may not receive their services.
This Maintenance Office does not have all the superintendents it needs. Buildings in the area could be more at risk.
This Maintenance Office desperately needs more superintendents. The safety of the buildings in the neighborhood depends upon it!
Only one superintendent works here. He's doing the best he can to care for buildings under the circumstances, but he can only do so much.
This Maintenance Office has no superintendents and isn't maintaining anything!
Fire! The superintendents neglected to maintain their own building, and won't service any of the building in the neighborhood unless the fire is put out!
The stonecutters are eager to start chiseling marble, but they either can't get to the quarry or there is no marble to be quarried.
The lumberjacks would gladly begin cutting down trees, but they either can't reach the forest or there are no trees.
The miners dream of mining silver, but they can't. Either something is blocking their path to the ore, or there isn't any ore.
The miners here cannot make their way to the copper ore! There must be some obstacle in the way! Or maybe there is no copper ore!
The hunters here are resting because either there isn't any more prey to be found or they can't reach the animals.
This Dairy has goatherds, but they can't find any goats. Either no goats live in the city, or the goatherds can't reach the pasture.
The shepherds are refusing to leave the Carding Shed. Either no sheep live in the city, or the shepherds can't access the pasture.
The miners here are anxious to mine orichalc ore. Unfortunately, either their path to the ore is blocked, or there is no ore.
The ranchers here would love to wrangle cattle. Regrettably, either no cattle live in the city or the ranchers can't reach them.
The Tax Office sends out clerks to squeeze tax money from the city's residents.
The clerk is walking the city's streets, collecting money from all the houses he passes.
The clerk is getting ready to step out into the street and start collecting taxes. He's just checking his hair one last time.
This Tax Office has all the clerks it needs to take the city's residents for all they're worth.
This Tax Office is missing a few clerks. A few precious drachmas are slipping through the cracks!
This Tax Office is understaffed, so some of the city's residents could be getting away with paying no taxes.
This Tax Office needs more clerks! It isn't collecting as many drachmas as it should be, and the Tax Office wrestling team is missing its heavy weight.
This Tax Office hardly has any workers. Only a few drachmas are being collected, and the clerk is getting lonely.
This Tax Office has no clerks to call its own and cannot collect any taxes.
All the clerks are lollygagging about. Without a Palace in the city, they cannot collect taxes.
This Tax Office isn't working because the Palace needs employees. With no supervisors around, the clerks aren't bothering to work.
Fire! The clerks here have abandoned their posts and won't collect any taxes unless the fire is extinguished!
This building is sanctified! The surrounding neighborhood will pay double their normal taxes!
People love oranges - it's like eating sunshine! Orange tenders from an Orange Tenders' Lodge pick the fruit when it's ripe.
You have called a halt to orange tending.
The next orange harvest is in
The Fountain's clean water is a favorite of residents of common housing.
This Fountain has no water bearers at all! Without workers, the local residents will have nary a drop of water to drink.
Only a few water bearers are working at this Fountain. If the glamorous life of carrying a jug of water doesn't draw more workers soon, people could soon go without water.
This Fountain is lacking several water bearers. Some residents could run out of water.
This Fountain is missing a water bearer or two. The remaining water bearers are doing what they can to make sure that residents are getting the water they need.
With a full staff of water bearers, this Fountain's water is reaching as many people as it can.
Workers from the Refinery mine mysterious orichalc ore and transform it into magical metal.
You have instructed orichalc metallurgists to stop mining.
The Refinery is quiet. With no workers, it cannot produce orichalc.
The full staff of metallurgists here is producing orichalc as quickly as possible.
This Refinery could use a few more metallurgists. Orichalc production has slowed a little.
This Refinery needs several more metallurgists. It is taking the staff a while to produce orichalc.
Only a few metallurgists work here. It is taking a long time to convert orichalc ore into metal.
This Refinery is barely operational. It needs many more workers to produce orichalc efficiently.
Fire! If a superintendent extinguishes the flames before the building collapses, the building will be saved.
Tender sprouts become full-grown stalks of wheat here.
You have shut the wheat industry off.
With no employees, the field is choked by weeds, and no wheat can grow.
This Wheat Farm has all the employees it needs to make sure that the wheat is healthy.
This Wheat Farm is short a few field hands. As a result, the harvest won't be quite as large as it could be.
This Wheat Farm is understaffed. A few areas of the field have not been sown at all, and the harvest will be considerably smaller than it could be.
This Wheat Farm has only a few farmhands. They have only sown a small portion of the field, and the harvest will be paltry.
This Wheat Farm has so few employees that it can barely operate. After the grim reaping, the farm will be lucky to produce even a sheaf of wheat.
This farmland has been damaged and nothing can grow on it. Perhaps its fertility will return with time.
The next wheat harvest is in
The Onion Farm produces the yummy vegetable that makes people weep for joy.
You have ordered onion production to stop. Now there's a reason to cry!
Not a single onion will be grown on this farm if it doesn't find any workers.
This Onion Farm has all the employees it needs to produce potent, pungent onions.
This Onion Farm is missing a few employees. The remaining workers are growing as many onions as they can, but the harvest will be a little smaller than average.
This Onion Farm needs more workers! Without enough employees, this Onion Farm cannot grow all the onions it could.
Only a few workers are tending the onions on this farm. With so few employees, the farm will harvest only a scanty amount of onions.
Barely anyone works on this Onion Farm. Only a handful of onions will be harvested from this farm.
This Onion Farm has been damaged, and no onions can grow here. Perhaps the land will one day become fertile again.
The next onion harvest is in
Crunchy carrots are born here on the Carrot Farm.
You have turned carrot production off.
This Carrot Farm has no employees. No little carrot tops will poke their heads from under the ground.
With a full staff, this Carrot Farm is as efficient as it can be.
This Carrot Farm is short a couple of employees. The farm's harvest won't be quite as large as it could be.
Without enough workers, this Carrot Farm is not planting its entire field. The harvest from this farm will be small.
This Carrot Farm only has a few employees. They won't be able to bring in a very large harvest at all.
This Carrot Farm has far too few employees. It's all they can do to keep the rabbits away. The harvest will be quite tiny indeed.
This Carrot Farm's land has been damaged somehow, and nothing can grow on it. The field may regain its fertility with time.
The next carrot harvest is in
All of the world loves olives! Growers from the Growers' Lodge pick olives off the trees at harvest time.
Farm damaged - needs time to recover.
The next olive harvest is in
Growers from the Growers' Lodge pick grapes from the vine. I bet you wondered how I knew that.
Land damaged - needs time to recover.
The next grape harvest is in
The Foundry sends out miners to collect copper ore and then smelts the ore into bronze.
You felt that smelting should halt.
This Foundry has no employees and is producing no bronze.
With a full staff, this Foundry is smelting away, producing bronze as quickly as it can.
This Foundry is missing a few employees, and bronze production has slowed a little.
This Foundry needs several more employees and cannot produce bronze very quickly at all.
This Foundry desperately needs more workers. Its productivity has plummeted.
This Foundry hardly has any workers at all. Bronze production has slowed to a crawl
The Shop sends out stonecutters to quarry a huge slab of marble which is then fashioned into usable slabs.
You have shut off marble quarrying.
All is quiet at the Shop. With no employees, marble cannot be quarried.
This Shop has all the workers it needs. It is producing marble as efficiently as it can.
This Shop is short a few workers. It is still chiseling out marble, but not quite as quickly as it could.
This Shop needs several more workers. Marble production has slowed considerably.
The few stonecutters working here are about to lose their marbles. They work hard all day but still manage to produce very little marble.
This Shop hardly works at all! It needs many more employees if it is to produce marble efficiently and quickly.
The Shop is waiting for a fresh block of marble from the quarry.
The Mint sends miners to collect silver ore and converts it into cold, hard drachmas.
You have turned off the Mint industry. Do you really mean to say no to free money?
All is quiet at the Mint. With no employees, silver cannot be turned into money.
With a full staff, coins are being minted from silver at peak efficiency.
This Mint could use a few more employees. Coin production has slowed a bit.
This Mint doesn't have enough employees. It is still operating, but it is making drachmas at a reduced rate.
With so few workers, it is taking a very long time to turn silver into drachmas.
This Mint is horribly understaffed. It will still produce drachmas, but it will take a very, very long time.
Workers at the Mint are waiting for miners to return with silver ore.
The Timber Mill sends lumberjacks out to cut down trees and then processes the logs into planks.
With no lumberjacks, this Timber Mill does not work.
All the lumberjacks are okay here and busily cutting down trees and hewing out planks.
This Timber Mill is short a lumberjack or two. The lumberjacks this mill does have are working their hardest, but timber production has slowed a little.
This Timber Mill is understaffed. The lumberjacks are cutting down trees very slooowly.
Not nearly enough lumberjacks work at this Timber Mill. Productivity is way down.
This Timber Mill needs many more lumberjacks to operate efficiently. It will still produce timber, but at a snail's pace.
At the Dairy, goatherds turn milk into glorious cheese.
You have turned the cheese-making industry off.
This Dairy has no goatherds. Any goats in the city are running free with udders full of milk.
This Dairy has all the goatherds it needs. Curds are separated from whey and made into cheese at maximum efficiency.
This Dairy doesn't quite have a full staff of goatherds. Some goats are missing their daily milking, and it is taking longer to make a full wheel of cheese.
This Dairy needs more goatherds. Cheese making is taking longer than it should.
This Dairy needs many more goatherds. Many of the goats in the field are swollen with milk, but the small staff here can only milk so many of them.
This Dairy is severely understaffed. Cheese production has slowed to a crawl.
Vintners crush grapes into wine, the favored beverage of the elite class.
This Winery has no employees. No wine can be made here.
This Winery has a full staff and is busy fermenting wine and pouring it into amphorae.
This Winery could use a few more workers. Wine is still being produced, but at a slightly reduced rate.
Productivity at this Winery is down. It needs more workers to crush grapes at maximum efficiency.
This Winery needs more vintners. Wine production has slowed, and the workers here may soon foment a revolt if they don't get more help.
The few workers here are beginning to whine. Without more help, it will take a long time before the wine is ready to be sold.
This Winery needs grapes in order to produce wine.
Shepherds from the Carding Shed cut fleece from sheep and prepare it for use.
You have ordered a halt to fleece production.
With no employees, the Carding Shed cannot operate, and any sheep in the area are roaming the countryside with long, flowing wool.
A full staff of shepherds means sheep are shorn of their wool at breakneck speeds.
This Carding Shed could use another shepherd or two. The Shed is still producing fleece, but not quite as quickly as it could be.
This Carding Shed needs more workers. Sheep are frolicking in the fields with no fear of losing their wool in the near future.
This Carding Shed needs many more workers, and fleece production has slowed considerably.
This Carding Shed has very few shepherds in its employ. The sheep can count on keeping their fleece for a while.
Here, bronze is cast into strong implements of war.
You have decreed that armor production cease.
Without any employees, no armor can be produced at this Armory.
This Armory has all the workers it needs and is rapidly producing armor.
This Armory could use another worker or two. Armor production has slowed slightly.
This Armory is understaffed. It is still producing armor, but at a much reduced rate.
This Armory doesn't have nearly enough workers. The workers that are here are working as hard as they can, but armor production has slowed considerably.
This Armory has very few employees. Armor is being produced here at an excruciatingly slow pace.
This Armory doesn't have enough bronze to produce armor.
Here, olives are crushed into olive oil, a favorite good of all citizens.
You have shut down the olive oil industry.
Without employees, this Olive Press can make no olive oil.
This Olive Press is fully staffed and making olive oil as quickly as it can.
This Olive Press needs a couple more workers. It is still making olive oil, but not quite as quickly as it could.
This Olive Press doesn't have all the workers it needs. Olive oil production has slowed.
With only a few workers, this Olive Press isn't producing very much olive oil at all.
This Olive Press barely has workers at all. It is taking quite some time for it to produce olive oil.
This Olive Press needs olives before it can make olive oil.
Here, bronze is melted and poured into molds to make gorgeous sculptures.
You have shut this industry down.
This Sculpture Studio is casting about for employees. With no employees, it can't make any sculptures.
This Sculpture Studio has all the artists it needs to make sculpture quickly and skillfully.
This Sculpture Studio needs a few more artists. It is taking a little longer than it should to craft fine sculptures.
This Sculpture Studio is understaffed. The artists are complaining about the conditions, and sculpture production has slowed.
This Sculpture Studio needs several more workers. It is taking some time for the artists here to produce high-quality art.
This Sculpture Studio needs many more artists. It is barely turning out any sculptures at all.
This Sculpture Studio doesn't have enough bronze to produce sculpture.
A clerk has not visited to collect the tax.
Residents here are happy with the city.
A few residents are dissatisfied with the city.
Residents grumble a little about the city.
Many of the residents are unhappy with the city.
Residents here are perturbed by city conditions.
Residents here are angry over city conditions.
Residents here are fuming over city conditions.
The residents of this home forage the land for food.
This house is devolving due to low appeal.
This house will devolve soon. It has lost access to clean water from a Fountain.
This house will devolve soon. There aren't enough cultural pursuits in the area
Unless more food is delivered soon, this house will soon devolve.
This house has lost access to a Stadium and will soon devolve.
There is no more wine in this house to drink. Unless some is delivered soon, the house will devolve.
This house is devolving. It needs some fleece.
This house will soon devolve if it does not receive more olive oil.
My household for a horse, the residents cry. This house will devolve soon if no horses are delivered.
If this house does not receive more armor, it will devolve.
The residents of this house have everything they need.
This house cannot evolve. Some of the buildings in the area have reduced the neighborhood's appeal.
This house cannot evolve until the appeal of the neighborhood increases.
The parched residents of this house need water if this house is to evolve.
This house needs access to more culture venues in order to evolve.
The residents of this house need food from an Agora before the house can improve.
The residents of this house need access to a Stadium before the house can evolve.
The residents of this house want wine to drink.
This house cannot evolve unless a peddler delivers some fleece.
This house cannot evolve until its residents receive some olive oil from a peddler.
This house needs horses to evolve.
The residents of this house can't make improvements until they receive some armor.
The residents of this house are making improvements right now.
This house is waiting for residents to move in.
This house will devolve soon. There isn't enough scientific activity in the area.
No curator has passed this house recently, and it will soon deteriorate.
Residents here need to be taught by more scientists before they'll improve their home.
A curator needs to teach the residents of this house before it can evolve.
If the residents here don't receive more chariots, their home will deteriorate.
Residents here won't improve their home unless they receive chariots from a peddler.
This plot is eagerly awaiting a resident to move in.
This plot is too far away from the road.

Nothing to trade here, just passing through
Deliverymen are getting and distributing goods.
Deliverymen are getting goods to export.
This granary cannot accept any more food.
Trade by sea is shut down for now
Trade by land is shut down for now
With no workers, this Sanctuary cannot function at all.
This Sanctuary lacks a few priests. No sacrifices can be conducted.
This Sanctuary is understaffed. There aren't enough priests to conduct sacrifices.
This Sanctuary has only a few priests. They have no time to conduct sacrifices.
This Sanctuary hardly has any workers. Its few priests do not have time to conduct sacrifices.
This Sanctuary is inactive. Sacrifices must be performed here for the Sanctuary to function.
Our priests are out looking for suitable sacrifices.
We are conducting sacrifices right now.
This Sanctuary is active for another
Our priests are not looking for sacrifices right now.
Zeus has heard your prayers! Zeus will personally trounce an invading army on your behalf!
Poseidon, in his vast kindness, has responded to your prayer! Your city will be blessed with an abundance of seafood!
Demeter has responded to your pitiful cry! She will provide you with a Granary's worth of food!
The shining Apollo has granted your request! He will cure all illnesses in the city!
The chaste huntress Artemis has heard your prayer! Soon, she will deliver a plentiful supply of meat to your Granaries!
The martial Ares has responded to your plea! He will go to war with your soldiers!
Your prayers have stirred Aphrodite's heart. If there is room, more people will soon call your city home!
The swift Hermes has heard your prayers! He will fulfill an outstanding request on your city!
The wise Athena has answered yes to your plea. A bounty of olives and olive oil will soon be yours!
Hephaestus has heard your prayers! His fearsome Talos will soon protect your city!
The festive Dionysus has granted your request! Wine will flow in your city!
Hades has heard your plea! Your coffers will soon grow!
Hera has heard your prayer! Soon, the city's Agoras will not want for supplies!
Atlas has responded to your cry! Artisans at a construction project will find that they have the supplies they need!
Zeus has heard your prayer to strike down an enemy, but can't fulfill it yet. He will when the opportunity presents itself.
Poseidon has heard your prayer, but you don't have the room for more food. He might fulfill your prayer later.
Demeter has heard your prayer, but you don't have the room for more food. She might fulfill your prayer later.
Apollo has heard your prayer to heal your sick, but can't fulfill it yet. He will when he gets an opportunity.
Artemis has heard your prayer, but she can't fulfill it. She'll fulfill your prayer later if she can.
Ares has heard your prayer and is eager to go to war with your soldiers. He will go at the next available opportunity.
Aphrodite has heard your prayer for more people, but can't fulfill it at this time. She might fulfill it later.
Hermes has heard your prayer, but there are no outstanding requests on your city. He might fulfill your prayer later.
Athena has heard your prayer, but you don't have the room for olive oil and olives. She might fulfill your prayer later.
Hephaestus has heard your prayer for Talos' aid, but can't fulfill it yet. He will at the next opportunity.
Dionysus has heard your prayer, but you don't have the room for wine. He might fulfill your prayer later.
Hades has heard your prayer for more money, but can't fulfill it yet. He will at the next opportunity.
Hera has heard your prayer for more supplies, but she can't fulfill it now. She will at the next opportunity
Atlas has heard your prayer, but he can't help you now. He will as soon as he gets the chance.
Zeus is in no mood to hear another one of your prayers. Maybe he'll be more agreeable later.
Poseidon distinctly remembers answering one of your prayers not too long ago. He won't grant you another blessing yet.
Demeter has denied your request. Don't pester her too often.
Apollo has ignored your plea. He cured your citizens not too long ago, and you can't always expect him to solve your health crises.
Artemis has refused you. She procured some meat not too long ago and doesn't want to overhunt the stock.
Ares has denied your prayer. He's still repairing the weapons he damaged from the last time he went to war for you.
Aphrodite loves you, but not enough to constantly answer your prayers. Do not ask her for too much.
Hermes has rejected your latest plea. He helped you not too long ago and can't always be there for you when you need him.
Athena won't grant you any olives or olive oil for the time being. Perhaps you should try again later.
Hephaestus is too busy in his forge to grant your request. Plus, he loaned you Talos not that long ago.
Dionysus is off partying somewhere and won't fulfill your prayer. You might have better luck later.
Hades has grumbled a surly 'No' to your prayer. He filled your coffers not too long ago.
Hera has screamed 'No' to your prayer. She restocked your Agoras not too long ago.
Atlas grumbled 'No' to your latest plea. He just helped your artisans a short time ago!
Zeus will protect your city from invaders in time of need.
Poseidon will haul in a catch of fresh seafood, if there is any in the city, when your citizens are hungry.
Demeter will harvest food for you when your citizens have none.
Apollo will cure what ails your city's residents.
If any prey animals live in your city, Artemis will hunt them on your behalf when your city needs food.
Ares will march to war with your soldiers.
Aphrodite will fill the city's houses with people when the city's housing is vacant.
Hermes will fulfill a request for supplies on your behalf.
Athena will make a gift of olives and olive oil when your city is lacking.
Hephaestus will instruct Talos to defend your city from harm.
Dionysus will provide wine from his very own casks when your city needs it.
Hades will deposit drachmas in your treasury when you need funds.
Hera will restock your Agoras when they are running low on supplies.
Atlas will help with a construction project.
Zeus' Stronghold will endow your city with an Oracle. Zeus himself will protect you from all other Olympians, and you will earn respect throughout the world for worshiping the Master of Olympus.

When you honor Poseidon with a Sanctuary, the Kraken will patrol your waters and defeat any adversaries he meets. Your fishermen and urchin gatherers will find their work easier and produce more food for the city.
The Garden of Demeter is surrounded by fertile fields, which can be planted with crops and support livestock.

By building Oracle of Apollo, you will be able to see the future by consulting the Oracle. Your city's contestants stand a greater chance of winning the pan-Hellenic Games, and Apollo will defend your borders against monsters.
Two companies of fierce Amazons reside within Artemis' Menagerie, and they will help you in any of your military pursuits. Artemis herself will hunt down predatory animals in your city.

With Ares' Fortress in the city, two savage companies of warriors will serve you as loyally as they serve Ares. Sometimes Ares' Dragon will even engage an enemy he spots during his forays around your city.

No one wants to leave a city that hosts Aphrodite. Emigration ceases when Aphrodite's Haven is in the city, and the city itself becomes more attractive. Aphrodite will also dissuade Hephaestus, Ares, Hermes and Dionysus from doing harm to your city should they invade.
When Hermes' Refuge is in the city, the city's deliverymen, inspired by his speed, move quicker. Traders, too, are speedier, and more of them come to your city.
The Arbor of Athena has a grove of olive trees from which you can make olive oil. Athena will also endow your soldiers with more strength, and she will defend your city from attack.
Hephaestus will grant you copper from his forge when there is a Sanctuary to him in the city. He will also protect your city's buildings from fire.
The Grove of Dionysus has a vineyard whose grapes can be harvested and made into wine. Dionysus will also lull any would-be outlaws into peacefulness.

Hades' three-headed hound, Cerberus, will patrol your city's streets, striking down enemies, when you build the Gates of Hades. Hades will also offer you the riches of the earth, silver ore, that can be converted into drachmas at a Mint.

The Orchard of Hera features stands of Orange Trees that can be harvested for food. She also blesses your people, making them more fertile. She keeps an eagle-eye out for her husband, Zeus, and protects your city from the king of all gods.
With the Pillar of Atlas in the city, black and white marble is more easily quarried. And, Artisans' Guilds send out more workers to any construction site.
If Zeus hears your prayer, he will reward you by striking down the next enemy army that invades your city.
Pray to Poseidon if you need more seafood for your city's residents.
If Demeter responds to your prayer, she will bring you a bounty of food.
If Apollo answers your prayers, he will remove all illness from the city.
If Artemis fulfills your prayer, she will provide you with meat.
Pray to Ares if you would like him to accompany your soldiers in far-off battle.
If Aphrodite answers your prayer, she will fill your city's homes with people.
If Hermes hears your prayer, he will fulfill an outstanding request upon your city.
A successful prayer to Athena yields olives and olive oil from her sacred grove.
When Hephaestus answers your prayers, he enlists Talos to defend your city, just as he defended Crete.
Dionysus rewards successful prayers with wine!
Pray to Hades if you want to witness an increase to your treasury!
Pray to Hera to see your Agoras restocked!
If Atlas answers your prayer, he'll help you with a construction project!
No suitable sacrifices are available. The priests will try again later.
This Modest Pyramid is admired for its beautiful lines and simple form.
This Pyramid brings a smile to the face of anyone who gazes upon it.
This towering Great Pyramid is a source of civic pride for all who live in the city.
This awe-inspiring Majestic Pyramid stretches toward the sky in a dazzling display of craftsmanship.
This Small Monument to the Sky is pleasing to the eye.
This Monument to the Sky reminds all who live in the city of the guiding stars that shine at night.
This Grand Monument to the Sky is the talk of the city. People hold their heads a little higher knowing that such an impressive edifice graces their city.
This Minor Shrine to [god] is a source of pleasure to both [god] and your people.
This gorgeous Shrine to [god] has granted both [god] and your people bragging rights over the world.
This stately Major Shrine to [god] has become a favorite topic of conversation for both [god] and the residents of this city. They enjoy talking about its perfect form.

The Pyramid of the Pantheon is a glorious achievement. The gods, impressed with your loyalty to the entire pantheon, have vowed never to act in anger towards you. The beauty of the Pyramid is an inspiration to all.
Stretching towards the gods' home, the Altar of Olympus is a sacred and holy place. Sacrifices conducted here are offered up to all the gods worshiped in the city.
The stunning Temple of Olympus serves as a reminder of the power and majesty of the gods.
Approaching the stars themselves, the Observatory Kosmika is a place of wonder and of science. Everyone in the city benefits from the observations that the astronomers make here.
The Museum Atlantika is a magnificent testament to the importance of learning. All who live in the city learn from the fantastic exhibits displayed here.
Construction has been halted. Artisans are finishing their current tasks, but will do no more work until construction is resumed.
Construction has been halted. Artisans will not return to the site until construction is resumed.
By building Oracle of Apollo, you will be able to see the future by consulting the Oracle, and Apollo will defend your borders against monsters.
Columns add a touch of class and increase the appeal of any neighborhood.
The beautiful Avenue is a pleasure to walk and to gaze upon.
The Boulevard brings grace to a neighborhood.
This nice bench provides a comfortable place for residents to rest their weary bones.
Residents take time out to stop and smell the roses here.
Residents like to picnic at the beautiful Gazebo.
People like to get lost in the twisting, turning Hedge Maze.
Both fish and people leap for joy when there is a Fish Pond in the city!
This graceful obelisk brings smiles to the faces of people everywhere.
People who live near Sun Dials are never late for anything, making them quite popular with their friends.
Who knew that a simple shrub could one day be a fantastic animal? Residents marvel at the amazing shapes that the gardeners create.
Perhaps Pegasus himself made this spring by striking the ground with his hooves! The splashing water makes people smile.
No one is sure what the significance of the stone circle is, but people like it just the same.
This impressive obelisk honors the natural beauty of rock.
Water Parks of all shapes and sizes delight everyone! It is the perfect place for the city's residents to splash and play.
This sculpture of the gleeful dolphin reminds the citizens of the wonders of the sea.
Round and round the planets go on the Orrery. It reminds your citizens of their proper place in the world!
Massive shells have been collected from Poseidon's ocean and fashioned into a nice garden.
A favorite Saturday night hang-out, residents love to splish splash in a neighborhood bath.
People love to watch birds play in a Bird Bath, and the birds welcome the opportunity to keep squeaky clean!
Military set to auto-defend the city. See Military tab or options menu.
This building, once a fine and beautiful structure, has been reduced to dust.
New calves are born, and old cows are turned into tasty meat here at the Corral.
This Corral has all the workers it needs, and it is operating at peak efficiency.
This Corral is a few workers short. Meat is still being produced, but at a slightly slower rate.
This Corral needs more workers. Meat production has slowed.
Only a few people work at this Corral. Cattle are kept in the field far past their prime.
This Corral hardly has any workers. Only a very small amount of meat is produced here.
With no employees, this Corral will never produce meat.
Fire! If a superintendent doesn't put out the flames in time, this Corral will be lost!
This Corral is cursed! The meat stored here has turned rancid, and no more will be produced until the curse is lifted.
This Corral is sanctified! The cattle have grown larger, and more meat than usual is being produced!
You have sent the ranchers home.
To trade these newly available goods, tell your Commerce Aide to set the goods' status to Import or Export.
REMEMBER! Your city needs a Pier before any trade ships can come into the city.
Click on a city to govern

This episode has no winning or losing conditions. It is about city building, pure and simple.

Here, hunters gather and prepare to kill their prey.
You have ordered that no more animals be hunted
With no employees, this Hunting Lodge cannot operate, and animals need not fear for their lives.
This Hunting Lodge has all the hunters it needs. Hunting parties are killing their quarry at an efficient pace.
This Hunting Lodge is short a hunter or two. A few animals are escaping the hunters' arrows, and meat production has slowed.
This Hunting Lodge is understaffed. Without enough hunters, it is producing much less meat than it could.
This Hunting Lodge needs many more hunters. Animals laugh at the paltry hunting parties, and hardly any meat is produced.
This Hunting Lodge hardly has any hunters and is not producing much meat at all.
The hunting party is in the field, stalking their quarry.
This Hunting Lodge is too far away from the breeding grounds. The hunters are refusing to walk that far.
The smell of barbecued meat is in the air, and the hunters have fled.
This Lodge is cursed! All the hunters here have perished, and any meat stored here has spoiled!
This Lodge is sanctified! The hunters can't miss, and the Lodge is producing four times as much meat as usual!
Walkers that roam the city turn around when they meet a Roadblock. Walkers with a specific destination in mind can pass right through.
Goddess of Warriors, Crafts and Wisdom
The urchin gatherers can not find a good place to dive for urchins.
The workers are preparing to hunt for sea urchins.
The urchin gatherers are wading through the water, headed for a spot known for its plentiful urchins.
The workers are busy collecting urchins.
You ordered a halt to urchin collecting.
Fire! All the urchin gatherers have left the building and won't resume the hunt for urchins until the fire is extinguished.
This building is cursed! A strong undertow has pulled the urchin gatherers under, and the Urchin Quay won't provide urchins to the city until the curse is lifted!
This building is sanctified! Urchins are suddenly very plentiful, and the gatherers are hauling in four times the usual amount!
The watchman is out making his rounds.
The watchman is making his last preparations before stepping out onto the city's streets.
This Watchpost has all the workers it needs. A full patrol of watchmen is walking the beat.
This Watchpost needs a few more workers. The Watchpost is still sending out patrols, but not as quickly as it could.
This Watchpost needs several more workers. It is sending out watchmen at a slowed pace.
This Watchpost needs many more workers if its watchmen are to help combat unrest.
This Watchpost barely has employees at all. It can send a watchman out very infrequently.
This Watchpost has no employees and is not sending out any watchmen at all.
Fire! The workers here are too busy watching their building burn to patrol the streets.
Something there is that doesn't love a Wall - enemy soldiers, for example. Walls help to keep invaders at bay.
And they're off! The Hippodrome is complete, and the horses have begun to run.
The track is incomplete. No horses will race here until you complete the circuit.
Few people attend the short races held at this tiny Hippodrome. The races are over almost as soon as they begin!

Citizens can be heard yelping excitedly, talking of the latest races at the track. They are quite pleased that the city has such an exciting form of entertainment and are spending money freely on refreshments.

Citizens can be heard yelping excitedly, talking of the latest races at the track. They are quite pleased that the city has such an exciting form of entertainment and are spending money freely on refreshments.

News of the thrilling races in your city has spread. Your allies have begun to attend the races, and are pleased to find your citizens hospitable. Foreigners and locals alike are buying refreshments and souvenirs, and your allies and your own citizens think more highly of you.

News of the thrilling races in your city has spread. Your allies have begun to attend the races, and are pleased to find your citizens hospitable. Foreigners and locals alike are buying refreshments and souvenirs, and your allies and your own citizens think more highly of you.

This impressive Hippodrome is earning the admiration of even your rivals! People from around the world are parting with their hard-earned cash here, and your popularity in your own city and around the world is on the rise.
It is considered a great honor to attend a race at this venerable Hippodrome. The Hippodrome has made your city the talk of the world, and your citizens are beaming with pride.

What is it with you and Hippodromes? While people from around the world are flocking to see the races at this gargantuan Hippodrome, your own citizens are choking on the dust raised by the pounding of horse hooves. Others in the world are impressed with your achievement, but your own citizens are not pleased.
This Hippodrome is cut off from the rest of the world, and no one can reach it!
You can only have one Hippodrome in the city! Either delete the other Hippodrome in the city, or connect it to this one.
The Hippodrome must be connected to a road to function properly.

horses from a Horse Ranch before races can begin. If the Hippodrome is connected to more than one road, the handler will choose which of the roads he wants to use to get horses. Make sure that the road he chooses is connected to a Horse Ranch.
The Gatehouse keeps good things in and bad things out.
Stonecutters from the Workshop chisel out huge blocks of black marble at a Black Marble Quarry and break it down into usable slabs.
You have decided to shut down black marble quarrying.
No one is employed at this Workshop. It is not producing any black marble.
This Workshop has a full staff. The stonecutters here are quarrying black marble at a highly efficient rate.
This Workshop is producing black marble, but not as quickly as it could be. The Workshop needs a few more workers.
Chiseling has slowed. This Workshop needs several more workers to operate at peak efficiency.
Only a few lonely stonecutters work here. They cannot produce much black marble without more help.
Black marble production is nearly at a standstill. This Workshop needs many more employees.
The stonecutters here are eagerly waiting for a huge block of black marble from the Quarry.
Without enough employees, this Tower will be helpless in the face of an attack.
Sentries stand ready to fire upon anyone encroaching on the city.
From the heights of the Tower, sentries can spot approaching attacks and help to defend the city.
Fire! The sentries have abandoned their post, and no one will guard the city from this Tower unless the fire is put out.
This Tower is cursed! The sentries here have become cowards, and they won't rediscover their bravery until the curse is lifted. Until then, the Tower won't defend the city.
You need a Palace to man towers.
You need a road connection to the Palace to man Towers.
Your city's Towers have orders to remain unmanned. Use the Military Tab to man all Towers.
To make this Tower even more deadly, import some Orichalc so that it can launch deadly Atlantean Fire upon invaders.
To enable this Tower to launch Atlantean Fire, mine and refine orichalc.
You are stockpiling orichalc, so none may be delivered to this Tower. Until orichalc is delivered, this Tower will not be able to launch Atlantean Fire.
The Tower is waiting for an Orichalc delivery, so that it may launch deadly Atlantean Fire against foes.
This Tower is equipped with a deadly Atlantean Fire launcher, which somehow employs mysterious orichalc.
Without employees, this Artisans' Guild cannot provide any artisans to help with building projects.
With a full staff of workers, the artisans here are ready to help with any big building project in the city.
This Artisans' Guild is a missing a few employees. The artisans are still available for building projects, but the projects may be slowed a little.
This Artisans' Guild needs more employees. This could hold up the city's building projects.
This Artisans' Guild has very few employees. It can only produce a few artisans for any building projects in the city.
This Artisans' Guild hardly has employees at all. It won't be able to provide many artisans to any building projects in the city.
Fire! The artisans are trying to salvage their tools and won't work on any building projects unless the fire is extinguished!
This Guild is cursed! The artisans are cowering inside, afraid of what might happen if they venture out!
This Guild is sanctified! The Guild will send out twice as many artisans as usual!
The trireme, the pride of the navy, moors at the Trireme Wharf between battles.
You have sent the trireme's crew home.
This Wharf has a full crew.
This Trireme Wharf is missing some workers.
This Trireme Wharf needs more workers.
This Trireme Wharf needs many more workers. The trireme cannot move without them.
This Trireme Wharf hardly has any workers at all. It cannot carry out any orders.
This Trireme Wharf is missing supplies. Make sure it has wood to build the ship and armor to outfit the marines.
The trireme is here at the wharf, waiting for orders.
The trireme is sailing the waters.
The trireme's crew is busy repairing the ship.
This Wharf needs a trireme! Make sure it has wood, armor, and enough workers to build it.
You need a Palace to commission triremes.
When it's not out on the high seas, the frigate docks at the Frigate Wharf.
You have sent the frigate's crew home.
This Wharf has a full crew.
This Frigate Wharf is missing some workers.
This Frigate Wharf needs more workers.
This Frigate Wharf needs many more workers. The frigate cannot move without them.
This Frigate Wharf hardly has any workers at all. It cannot carry out any orders.
This Frigate Wharf is missing supplies. Make sure it has wood to build the ship and armor to outfit the marines.
The frigate is here at the wharf, awaiting your command.
The frigate is sailing the waters.
The frigate's crew is busily repairing the ship.
This Wharf needs a frigate! Make sure it has wood, armor, and enough workers to build it.
You need a Palace to commission frigates.
Import Orichalc so that this frigate can build a deadly Atlantean Fire launcher.
This frigate can build an Atlantean Fire launcher if you produce some orichalc at a Refinery.
You are stockpiling orichalc. This frigate will not be able to shower Atlantean Fire on enemies until it receives some orichalc.
As soon as some orichalc is delivered to this Wharf, its frigate will be able to rain hot Atlantean Fire on its foes.
This Wharf's frigate has an Atlantean Fire launcher, made by our scientists with mysterious orichalc.
