All materials needed for this project have arrived
Artisans are placing the marble that forms the core of the Pyramid.
Artisans are placing the black marble that forms the core of the Pyramid.
Artisans are putting the finishing touches on the completed course, polishing the marble to a fine sheen and adding orichalc decorations.
Artisans are putting the finishing touches on the completed course, polishing the black marble to a fine sheen and adding orichalc decorations.
Crafty artisans are building an ingenious wooden Lift to haul supplies up the side of the Pyramid.
Artisans are toiling away, making sure that the top of the Pyramid is as beautiful as the rest of the structure.
Growers from the Growers' Lodge harvest fruit from the orchards.
You have ordered that no fruit be picked at all.
This Growers' Lodge has no employees. Any olive trees and vines in the city are growing wild.
This Growers' Lodge has all the workers it needs. Orchards are well-pruned, and fruit is harvested at peak efficiency.
This Growers' Lodge needs more workers. Fruit is not harvested as quickly as it could be.
This Growers' Lodge is understaffed. Some of the plants in the orchard are not being cared for properly, and fruit picking has slowed.
Only a few growers work here. The orchards are unkempt, and it's taking quite some time for the workers to complete the harvest.
This Growers' Lodge hardly has any workers. The small staff picks fruit as quickly as it can, but it's taking a very long time for fruit to be harvested.
The growers are out looking for ripe fruit to harvest.
This Growers' Lodge has employees, but there are no vines or olive trees for them to tend.
Fire! All the growers have gone home, and no more fruit will be harvested unless the fire is extinguished.
The growers can't reach the orchard. Something is blocking their path!
Orange tenders from the Orange Tenders' Lodge pick the sweetest, most succulent oranges for people to eat.
You've told all the orange tenders to go home.
No one works at this Lodge. Any Orange Trees in the city are laden with unpicked oranges.
This Lodge is fully staffed. Orange Trees are healthy, and oranges are picked when they are fully ripe.
This Lodge needs a few more growers to complete its ranks. Oranges are still picked, but not as many as usual.
More workers are needed here. Some oranges are going to waste, and a few trees aren't being tended.
This Lodge needs many more workers. Many of the oranges are becoming overripe before they can be picked.
This Lodge hardly has any workers. Only a few oranges are picked at their peak.
Orange tenders are out taking care of trees and picking ripe oranges.
This Lodge has workers, but there are no Orange Trees for them to maintain.
Fire! The orange tenders have deserted their posts and won't pick more oranges until the fire is out.
This Lodge has been cursed! Until the curse is lifted, the tenders will pick no oranges.
This Lodge has been sanctified! Oranges have suddenly become more plentiful!
The workers here can't reach the Orange Trees. Something must be blocking their path!

A new city, a new home, and maybe a new job! Fortune is smiling upon me!
Word is that there are plenty of jobs here for anyone who wants one.
I've heard that there's a cornucopia of food here! Yummy!
Looks like I'm leaving just in time!
There aren't enough jobs here. It's not that I mind not working, but a guy's gotta eat.
As Zeus is my witness, I'll never be hungry again! Some other city has got to have enough food to feed everyone.
Taxes are too high here. The price of citizenship just isn't worth it.
Wage? You call that a wage? I've had enough of you and your city, you cheapskate!
People are crammed into this city! Would it kill you to build some more housing, or are you trying to get people to leave?
You've mustered me from the comfort of my own bed one too many times! I'm going to find another place to live!
I think its reasonable to expect to be paid for one's services, but you seem to like to spend your money on other things! Goodbye!
I don't have anywhere to hide! Help!
Thrown out of my home!! What did I do to deserve this?
I've searched high and low, and there's not so much as a hut for me to call home. Not even Diogenes' barrel!
Havoc is going to be wreaked all over this city!
First, you make me live in a dumpy house and now I can't find a job! If I have to live like this, then these rich snobs should have to, too!
No food and a cruddy house! If I have to suffer, I'm going to make sure I don't suffer alone!
You take too much money from me and make me live in squalor. I'm going to take a little back!
I'm sufferin' along, earning next to nothing, livin' in a slum, while the rich get to play all day. I'll show them how the other half lives!
You call me from my run-down home to stand in a muddy field, armed with only a rock, to await an attack. And you wonder why I'm angry!
It's bad enough that I live in a ramshackle house, now you're not paying me! Someone's going to pay, and it might as well be the rich!
Maybe if I lived in a better house and had a job, I wouldn't steal!
I need food. If the Agora won't deliver any to me, I'll just take from those that have plenty. Oh, and I could use a better house, too!
You take most of my money, and all I get is a meager house in return. I'm going to take a little back!
Procrustes, Sciron, Autolycus. I'll add my name to this list of famous thieves if I can't earn better wages and find a good place to live!
Lots of things make me nervous. Mustered soldiers. A shoddy house. Know what happens when I get nervous? I steal!
My house is a mess, and you haven't paid me for weeks! I'll take what I can't afford to buy!
I don't mean to be bad. I'm just miffed because I don't have a job or a nice house to go home to!
I'm hungry, and I live in a shabby home. Mugging is the only thing that brings a smile to my face!
I'm tired of paying you a high tax on my cheap house, and I'm going to deal with my anger the only way I know how!
Let's see how you react to earning next to nothing and living in a squalor. I bet you'd start mugging, too!
While you're busy constantly mustering soldiers, I'm going to leave my horrible home and mug someone!
If you're going to make me live like a rat and not pay me, I'll find some nice, rich-looking person to mug!
Better houses and more jobs! That's what we need!
Hey! I'm hungry and my house is a mess! What are you going to do about it?
The high taxes and cheap housing in this city are making me mad! Maenad mad!
Give me a raise! I can't live on this pittance you call a wage, and I certainly can't afford to live in a better house!
You're always mustering soldiers, and I could be next. I suffer enough because of my run-down home, I don't want to go to war, too!
Do you really think you can force people to live in rickety houses and not pay them for their services? I've never seen such hubris!
Hygiene conditions in this city are atrocious! Even people in the rich, upscale part of town I live in are afraid of falling ill!
I don't know what the lower classes are complaining about. I've got plenty of food!
Commoners are littering the streets like old, discarded amphorae shards. You really should see to it that they find some employment.
How can it be that I, a member of the elite, live in a city that is subject to another? Say the word, and I'll attack our rival!
Send me to our rebelling subject! I'll make them see the error of their ways!
I do my part to bring the quality of this city up, but I can only do so much. There are things here that could be improved.
You have done a wonderful job building this city! I'm sure the Elysian Fields pale in comparison.
Gadzeus! I'm getting out of here!
Take that, you rapscallion! Theseus couldn't brook outlaws, and neither can I!
People here aren't very healthy at all. Maybe they need more pork!
I'm as hungry as the Horses of Diomedes! I need food!
A lot of poor saps are out of work, but not me! I am far too good a boar hunter to ever be unemployed!
When I think of how we're subjugated, it just makes me want to kill more boar!
A rebellion! Who would dare rebel against a city that boasts such talented boar hunters!?!
This city isn't the best, but it's all right overall.
This is the best city ever! The boar here are the best this side of Calydon!
There's boar around here somewhere. I can smell them!
This poor boar never stood a chance! My aim is as good as Atalanta's!
Hygiene conditions are terrible, and people could get sick. I just hope that it doesn't spread to my sheep!
I'm so hungry! I wonder what meadow tastes like. The sheep sure seem to like it!
Perhaps you should herd up the unemployed and drive them out of town!
I hate living in a vassal city. My sheep deserve to be in a mighty city that pays tribute to no one!
One of our colonies is trying to leave the fold! I'm glad that all my little sheep are loyal!
The sheep aren't thrilled to live here, but they don't have much choice.
I'd follow you anywhere! It's sheer pleasure living in a city like this!
No wolf will catch my sheep. Not while I'm here, anyway!
Soon, my fuzzy little sheep will be completely bald!
Stand still, Bessy. I don't want to cut you!
I love the smell of freshly cut fleece in the morning!
This city could use a visit from Asclepius! People aren't feeling well, and a plague could break out!
Being surrounded by cheese all day only makes me hungrier! Now I know how Tantalus feels!
Satyrs alive! I've never seen so many people out of work before!
When are we going to shake off the shackles of vassalhood?
I can't believe one of our subjects dares to rebel against us! Where's the gratitude?
This city is so so. Good enough for a goat, I suppose.
Every man and goat for himself! Run!
I'm as vigilant as Argos guarding Io! No wolf is going to get my goat!
These goats won't milk themselves! If they did, I'd be out of a job!
Milking goats isn't glamorous, but people need their cheese.
Drat! I spilt some milk. No use crying over it, I suppose.
People here are looking a little down around the gills. If hygiene conditions don't improve, a plague could break out!
I'm hungrier than Erysichthon! I wonder if anyone would notice if I made off with some fish!
There are more unemployed people here than there are fish in the sea!
Are we men, or are we guppies? Let's rise up against our rival!
So, one of our subjects thinks they're big enough not to need our protection. We'll see about that!
You could improve things, but it's not too terrible living here.
This city is a great catch!
Salty air, fish, and my cronies: that's all I need! Maybe if I'm lucky, I'll meet some Nereids!
All hail my hearty crew! Poseidon will soon wonder where all his fish went!
Catch the fish . . . You missed. Catch the fish . . . You missed again!
Cast the net wide, mateys! But, keep an eye out for Triton! We don't want to catch him!
City hygiene is atrocious! At least I've got sea breezes to keep me fit!
I wish I was like Proteus and could shape-shift into something that needed less food! I'm starved!
People used to do everything they could to find work. Now they just sit around waiting for someone to hand them a job on a silver platter!
Being a vassal makes me so angry! I'd like to stab our rival with an urchin spine!
Who would dare to rebel against our city? No more urchins for them!
What do I think of this city? It's okay, but I could probably teach you a thing or to about running a city!
I can't think of a single thing to complain about! This city is all right!
It's time to make myself scarce!
Collecting urchins is hard work, but someone has to do it!
I hope the people understand just how lucky they are to have such fine urchins!
People are whining that they don't feel well. If city hygiene doesn't improve soon, a plague could break out!
Maybe being hungry is simply what the Fates have in store for me, but I do wish I had more food!
Jobs are scarce. A lot of people are out of work.
Our city is better than our rival's! Why is it that we pay tribute to them?
One of our colonies has picked a fight with us. How rude!
This city isn't the pits, but it could be better.
Herodotus is sure to record this city as the best, and you as its greatest ruler!
Run! It may be too late for me, but save yourself!
Maybe one day, branches from this olive tree will be made into a wreath to crown a great champion!
Give the tree a gentle little shake, and ripe olives go plop plop onto the ground!
I can only imagine Oeneus' joy when Dionysus gave him the first vine. Vines are so fun to work with!
These succulent grapes are sure to make some fantastic wine!
Judging by people's ashen expressions, hygiene in the city is very bad.
These hunger pangs are sawing me in half! I need some food!
A lot of people are out of work, but I'm a lumberjack so I'm okay.
Being a vassal stinks! We should be subject to no one!
One of our subjects is trying to gain its independence. Hah!
This city's problems aren't as difficult to solve as the riddle of the sphinx, but things aren't perfect here.
This city has entered its halcyon days! It's a joy to live here!
I should make like a tree and leave! It's not safe here!
I just hope that the tree I cut down is just a tree and not a tree nymph, Daphne, or an oak capable of prophecy! My job isn't easy!
Boy, this wood is heavy, eh? What I need is a team of oxen!
I've never seen so many sick people before! I hope a plague doesn't break out!
Maybe if I swallowed a rock like Cronus did, I wouldn't be so hungry!
This city's unemployment issue is not Gordian Knot, but it's still a problem to be solved!
I'm tired of some other city meddling in our affairs!
One of our subjects thinks it has the mettle to break free from us!
This city lacks a little luster, but it's all right.
I've really taken a shine to this city!
I'll mine copper later! It's time to bolt!
Pick ax? Check. Bag? Check. I'm ready to mine!
This is the finest copper ore I've ever seen! Hephaestus would be pleased to work with it!
This is the finest copper ore I've ever seen! Hephaestus would be pleased to work with it!
I'd better take one of the coins from the Mint. Everyone is sick, and if plague breaks out, I might need it to pay Charon!
Hades' hounds! My stomach is growling louder than Stentor's voice! I need food soon!
Maybe you should ostracize some of the unemployed! At least then they wouldn't be hanging around doing nothing!
Our city is like the finest silver, valuable and pure. Why are we a vassal?
A subject is rebelling? I say let the ingrates go!
This city is fair to middling.
This city is as sweet as the Sirens' song, and you deserve to be an immortal, oh great leader!
Smelt! I'd better head for the hills!
There's silver in them there hills, and I'm going to get me some!
Soon, this ore will become cold, hard coin!
Soon, this ore will become cold, hard coin!
If city hygiene doesn't improve soon, Hades could soon have a lot more company!
I feel like someone has quarried everything out of my stomach, leaving a big empty pit behind.
There are a lot of sedimentary folks in this city who could use a job.
Being a vassal is terrible! Where is our pride?
I think our rebelling subject will find that it's not so easy to cleave yourself away from something as mighty as our city.
This city has its troubles, but not as many as were in Pandora's Box!
This city is superb! I hope I live as long as Nestor to enjoy it, and I hope you live as long as Nestor to rule it!
Ignacious! If I'm spotted, I could be killed!
I can't wait to get to the quarry and hew out a perfect piece of marble!
I need the strength of Hercules to haul this load back to the lodge!
I'm so sick, and I fear the worst. Persephone, I'll be paying you a visit soon!
I'd settle for the sword of Damocles hanging over my head, just as long as I can be at a banquet table with lots of food. I'm famished!
The unemployed are lollygagging about on the streets, getting in the way. I hate clutter.
A city as refined as ours should not have to pay tribute to some other city!
That rebelling city is disrupting things. Sometimes, the best thing to do with a project gone awry is to simply cut it off.
This city is okay, but it's lacking a certain panache, a certain je ne sais quoi.
In both form and function, this city cannot be matched by any other! And you, as its artificer, should be praised!
So magnificent! So sublime! So...dangerous! Aaaaaargh!
Off I go to the sanctuary to display my refined craft!
Soon, everyone in the city will admire my work! They'll say that I'm better than Daedalus!
This project is sure to be my finest achievement! Future generations will be in awe of my skill!
Only an artisan with my refined skills could build such a beautiful building!
I can't wait to pick out the finest food for my customers! I wouldn't want them to go hungry!
I don't know what my assistants are complaining about! These parcels are feather-light!
Baa baa. Nothing would hurt a little sheep, right? Baa baa.
We need fleece! Time to buy some more!
Careful! This fleece is for the Agora. If you rippa dese, you menda dese!
I'm just going to slip away now!
More olive oil coming right up!
All of my customers will love this olive oil!
Look who's here to share a cup...oh, wait. Aaaaaaaargh!
I'm going out to buy more wine! My customers really drank up quickly!
If the city would just stop spinning for a minute, I'm sure I could make it back to the Agora with this wine!
I'm going out to find the finest Corinthian helmets, not to mention hoplons, greaves and cuirasses!
Hephaestus couldn't make better armor than this! I hope it sees some action soon.
Why couldn't one of these horses be Pegasus? I'd love to be able to fly out of here!
What am I going to buy? A horse! It's a horse, of course, of course!
Move along, little doggies! These well-bred horses are sure to be exactly what my demanding customers want!
This city is, like, going to get totally shredded, dude.
So, the vendor was like, 'Hurry up with those goods,' and I was like, 'Whatever!' The vendor is such a doric!
Dude! Look at the size of this basket!
Did you see that wrestling match last night? Milo, like, totally smacked down his opponent! Nike must have been on his side!
Getting hurt is not what I bargained for! I'm skedaddling!
I need more supplies from the Agora! My customers are so demanding!
My cart is too light! It's time to load it up again with more goods!
Just once, I'd like the Agora to bring goods to me!
Get your genuine, first quality, Agora-approved goods here! No imitations!
What was it you wanted, Mrs. Pericles? Ah yes, here you go!
The quicker I can distribute these supplies, the quicker I can take a load off!
Yikes! Let's see how fast this cart will go!
I'm not moving until you hire someone to help me unload these goods!
The only room for these goods in the city is right here in my cart.
Who am I, Odysseus? How much longer do I have to travel before I reach my destination? And where's Calypso?
No workshops can accept these goods, and now my own Storehouse can't take them back. There's nothing to do but wait.
I'm not the only visitor here! Oh, no!
Are you talking to me? You and your city are scum, and everyone hates you!
Maybe, if I come here often enough, you'll offer me citizenship in your fine city!
I'm getting out of here post haste. I don't want to be here when the monster arrives!
You should be careful! An opposing god is on the way!
Word is that your city is about to be attacked!
Greetings! You'll be thrilled when you see what I have to trade with you!
It was a pleasure doing business with you! I hope Aeolus guides us home!
Right-click me? I'll right-click you! You and your city stink, and I only trade with you because my leader demands me to!
Coming here is like coming home! You and this city are so well-loved!
I heard some rumblings just outside your city. Either I ate some bad moussaka, or there's a none-too-happy god!
Just between you and me, I think there's a monster headed your way.
You'd better get ready for company! An enemy army is on its way here!
Our journey was long and arduous. What do you have for me?
Back and forth, back and forth. I'm beginning to feel like Sisyphus!
Sweet Melpomene! City hygiene is terrible, and a plague could soon erupt like the applause that erupts for me when I perform!
I'm so hungry! I'm just a shadow of my former self!
If nothing is done soon, I'll have my pick of extras. There are so many people out of work!
How could the best actor in all of Greece live in a city that is subservient to another?
Can it be true? A subject rebelling against our city, the home of the greatest actor in Greece?
This city falls just below my very exacting standards!
The finest actor in all of Greece lives in its finest city led by its finest ruler! This is the stuff of Homerian epics!
We could really use a deus ex machina right about now! I'll run in the meantime!
Off I go to thrill all of Greece with my emoting!
I won the games! I'd like to thank my teachers at the Drama School, the Muses and all the little people that made this possible.
How could this be? Second at the games! The contest must have been rigged!
It's a sad day in Greece when fine acting such as mine is ridiculed! Have we become barbarians?
If I weren't so strong, I'd be worried about catching something. Hygiene conditions in this city are poor.
I'm beginning to lose my manly muscles! I need food if I'm going to maintain my Adonis-like body!
Look at all those flabby idlers without jobs! They should get in shape and find jobs!
I'd like to smash the city we're paying tribute to! Smash it to smithereens!
I don't think it will be long before we have that city rebelling against us pinned and crying mentor.
This city could be in much better shape!
This city is like Ajax, head and shoulders above the rest!
I'm going to the games empty handed, but I'll come back with prestige and a wreath!
I won the games! I'm so great that I bet the gods will turn me into a constellation!
I would have won the games, but I had a cold that day. Still, second place isn't bad, right?
How did I do at the games? I don't want to talk about it.
A lot of people in the city are sick. I could break them like a twig!
Why are people complaining about no food? I ate just last week!
All these extra workers aren't doing the city any good.
Vassal, shmassal. We're stronger, we're faster and we shouldn't be paying tribute to anyone!
They should let me at that rebelling city! I'll show them a thing or two!
I'm sure there are worse places in Greece.
I bet Atlas is pleased to hold the sky up for this city! It's superb!
I'm going to the games! I can't wait to vanquish my opponents!
Everyone bow down before me! I won the games! I am the strongest man in all Greece, probably even stronger than Hercules!
I'm not quite sure how it happened, but I came in second place in the games! Someone must have cheated!
The sun was in my eyes! How can I perform well with the sun in my eyes?
It's academic: plague will certainly strike if hygiene conditions don't improve!
It's certainly hard to practice epicureanism in this city! What I wouldn't give for a plate of food!
Some of the peripatetic people should find themselves some employment!
We must be stoic in times like these. It's difficult to be a vassal, but perhaps one day we'll overthrow our rival!
We must be getting sophist...I mean soft! One of our subjects is rebelling against us!
This city's problems aren't just rhetorical, but it could be worse.
This city truly is a utopia! And I can't think of a wiser leader!
Thus, it becomes clear that the meaning of life is...aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaargh!
I'm off to the games! Soon, everyone will know just how smart I am!
I am so smart! So very, very smart! I won the games!
I took second place at the games. But what does it mean to win, anyway? Aren't we all winners in our way?
I lost the games! I guess it's true what they say: true genius is misunderstood in its own time!
I might be sacrificed myself if I don't get out of here!
I'm looking for a goat that's shaggy - but not too shaggy - and plump - but not too plump! Preferably one that's not a god in disguise!
Don't be frightened, little sheep. I won't hurt you...much.
Stop your bleating! You're making me feel guilty!
Great things are in store for you, little sheep. Trust me!
Sometimes, the gods want nothing but cattle as sacrifice. I hope I find a cooperative animal!
Don't get any ideas about stampeding over me. You are off to bigger and better things!
You'll just be ignored like Cas- sandra. That may be, but I must try to warn my leader. I foresee plague if hygiene conditions don't improve!
Pick up your side, I'm doing all the work! I'm doing my best. I'm so hungry! Well so am I, but I'm not complaining!
Workers, workers everywhere and not a job to take. If water-carrying weren't a two-person job, I'd be out of work, too!
Being a vassal is all wet! We deserve so much better!
I hope we give that rebelling city the what for! Yeah! They should know better than to rebel against us!
I guess I don't mind living here. It's better than a finger in the eye!
This city is more refreshing than any other city! And its leader is the most sparkling!
Hippocrates' oath! Hygiene is very poor here. Even I'm feeling under the weather!
More food is the only panacea to this city's hunger problems!
I hope the unemployed find jobs soon. It's not good for the psyche or the body.
When I think about being a vassal, I can feel my bile rising.
I think we should amputate the rebellious city!
When I tell you the news, just don't treat me like Achilles treated Copreus and kill the messenger. This city has problems.
This city is in the best shape, and you look marvelous, too!
Judging by what I see, I could be really busy, really soon.
Even Hygieia would have trouble curing this city! Hygiene conditions are very poor, and a plague could break out!
People here are in much worse condition than the buildings. I hope a plague doesn't break out!
I'm so hungry! Forget the buildings - I'm about to collapse!
A lot of people are down on their luck and out of work.
I just burn inside when I think about being a vassal!
So, one or our subjects is rebelling! I say we should tear it down!
This city needs a little work around the edges!
Eureka! I've found the best city and the most capable ruler!
If you're going to talk to me while I'm putting out a fire, you might as well make yourself useful. Why don't you go find me some water?
This is hot work! I feel like I'm in Hephaestus' forge!
I'm not so sure that Prometheus did us a favor when he gave us fire!
Some of these buildings are in terrible shape! I'd better get cracking!
Have you ever seen more solid buildings? Archimedes would be proud!
It's not time to talk, it's time to run!
Unrest in the city? Shut your mouth! Things are peaceful here!
Just the facts: rumor has it that there's a little unrest here, but it's nothing that I can't handle.
There's a lot of unrest in the city, so let's be careful out there!
This outlaw will regret that he ever got out of bed this morning. Book 'em, Plato!
People can't pay their taxes when they're dead, and many people might soon be if hygiene conditions aren't improved!
I'm so hungry. It takes all the joy out of collecting taxes!
The unemployed get so upset when I ask them to pay their taxes. I wish they'd find jobs!
Being a vassal is taxing my patience!
Our subject should heed Ixion's cry! They should show gratitude to us, their benefactor, and cease this rebellion!
I'll be laconic here: this city is mediocre at best.
I bet Mt. Olympus isn't as nice as this city!
I spy a problem...a BIG problem!
There are no enemies here now, but I'm ready just the same!
My quiver is full of arrows, and I'm ready for a fight!
Either the enemy is weak, or these arrows belong to Procris! I can't miss!
We may need a little luck to win this fight! May Tyche see us through!
I know trouble when I see it, and I see some right there!
My horse is getting restless! I hope an enemy comes soon!
My nerves are steeled, ready for the impending attack.
That enemy doesn't stand a chance against me! I'm as mighty a horseman as Bellerophon!
Hippolyte's girdle! The enemy looks mighty strong!
Trouble like that is just part of life in Greece!
I hope we see some action soon. This armor is starting to get itchy.
My armor is as good as Achilles'. I'll be ready when the invasion starts!
These foes aren't putting up much of a fight! We'll soon be victorious!
This enemy is tough! If we manage to win, it might be a Pyrrhic victory at best!
I knew I'd see some incredible sights when I left home, but that's more than I bargained for!
I'm bored. How much longer do I have to stand here?
When our enemy gets here, I'll bring new meaning to Clashing Rocks!
You call that an invasion? My grandmother could defeat that army!
The enemy is too fierce! Why couldn't I stay at home and be a deliveryman like mother wanted!
That's a titanic problem if I've ever seen one!
There aren't any enemies on our waters right now, but we're ready for them when they do come!
Trim that sail! Look lively! Enemies are coming, and we must be ready!
My crew is as skilled as Argonauts! It won't be long before this enemy is dead in the water!
It'll take all of my crew's skill to defeat this formidable opponent!

Chariots, formidable in any battle, are made here and paired with lightning-fast horses.
You have told all the chariot makers to go home.
This Chariot Factory will never produce chariots until it finds some employees!
With a full staff of chariot makers, this Factory is making chariots quickly.
This Factory is missing a worker or two. Chariots aren't being made as efficiently as they could be.
This Factory doesn't have all the workers it needs. Chariots are still being made, but at a slower pace.
Only a few people work at this Factory. It's taking the workers a very long time to finish a chariot.
Barely anyone works at this Factory. The production of a finished chariot is an infrequent event.
If chariots are to be produced here, this Factory needs a supply of wood, horses or both!
This building has chariots, but needs horses to pull the mighty war machines.
This building has chariots, but needs horses. Unfortunately, there are no horses nearby.
I was telling the wife just the other day how satisfying it is to sanctify buildings.
Have you forgotten my power, fellow Olympian? I'll remind you of it now!
I am Zeus. You have offended me, mortal, and you shall pay!
See what happens when you anger the king of the gods? Feel the full force of my wrath!
Urchins, fish and horses are in my domain, and multiply when I bid them to.
You have made me your enemy, and I shall make the sea your enemy too!
With this seafood, you can feed your hungry people!
With my blessing, the metal here shall multiply, whether it be silver, bronze or coin!
You have raised my ire, you pernicious cur!
I can't have you running out of drachmas, now can I? Please accept these with my compliments!
With my blessing, farms will yield a bumper crop!
At my bidding, fertile farmland will no longer be fertile, and all that is growing upon it will die!
May your Granaries be filled with this nourishing food!
The buildings that I bless will produce an abundance of goods!
Become barren at my touch, my olive trees.
I am pleased to provide this city with my favorite fruit and this wholesome oil.
I am Athena Polias, protector of the city! Suffer the consequences of your evil intent!
I will endow some hunters with my steady aim, and they will bring back an excess of meat!
By my skillful hand, the prey fell, and now succulent meat will feed your citizens.
Any animal I choose to hunt doesn't stand a chance against me!
I love watching mortals at their games, play acting, pontificating and athleticizing!
No more succor for the mind or the body shall be offered to the natives of this land!
Be well, fair mortals. You're not ready for the Underworld yet!
The scientists here are very smart, but I can make them even smarter!
Come along, my fire-breathing friend. Maybe we can find something for you to attack!
Feel the edge of my blade, you detestable sloth!
I am in my element! I can't wait to get on the battlefield and face off against the enemy!
With my blessing, bronze will more easily bend to the will of the workers!
At my command, all forges in the city will cool, and bronze will be unworkable!
Talos is performing well! Look how frightened the enemy is!
I like nothing better than seeing scores of mortals worshiping me!
Come with me, my sweets, come with me. Help me make this city pay!
With my blessing, let this house be filled with people!
Now, now, dear heart. Leave these nice little mortals alone!
Time for my morning constitutional! It's the best way to keep in shape!
This city will be hurting for money!
Dear mortal, I will fulfill a request from another city for you! Your stocks of the item won't be touched!
Look at all these happy people! More wine would make them even happier!
Life with me is so much fun! Leave this rotten city behind, my mortal friends, and give life with Dionysus a try!
This could be my best vintage yet! Drink up!
City hygiene is very poor here. Ordi- nary mortals are falling ill left and right, but I'm strong enough to withstand petty illnesses.
Being hungry reminds me of the old days when Chiron would cook for me. He used to make the best moussaka!
We should load up some of these extra workers into a wooden horse and send them on their way!
I'm not used to being under the yoke of someone else! I'm far too noble to be a vassal!
I have a nice horse I'd like to give to the rebelling city.
This city could be worse. At least it has me!
You may not be a hero like me, but you do know how to lead a city! Who needs Olympus when you can live here!
Looks like you've got yourself some trouble there!
Hephaestus really did do a wonderful job on this armor. With a monster on the way, it's going to see some action!
I pity those sniveling, sick weaklings. You should really do something to make them better.
I could really go for the golden apples of the Hesperides right about now. I'm so hungry!
I could probably think of some adventures to send the unem- ployed on if the city isn't inter- ested in finding them some work!
Being a vassal is worse than any of the twelve labors! I'm so much better than this!
It's foolish to rebel against a city that features me as one of its residents!
This city is no great shakes, but it's better than cleaning out the Augean Stables.
You've done something someone doesn't like very much!
A few more arm curls, and I'll be ready for the monster!
If I come down with anything, I'm sure Medea will cure me. Too bad the rest of the citizens don't have such an able spouse!
I'm so hungry. I wish I had some of Chiron's moussaka right now! He made the best moussaka!
If I ever wanted to get the Argo back in the water, this city has plenty of people available to make a nice crew.
I haven't been under someone's thumb like this since I left Lemnos!
The rebellion will show just who is powerful and who isn't!
It's no Iolchus, but this city is okay.
I was telling the wife just the other day how great a city this is!
You're going to need some help dealing with that!
Once Medea is done with the potion I need, I'll be ready to fight the monster!
Look at all the sick people! I wonder if the Lotus Eaters could prescribe something.
Maybe I should get Circe to come and change some people to swine. At least then there would be food!
Look at all the jobless people!
I am King of Ithaca, yet I'm a vassal here. How can that be?
Someone should put that rebelling city in its place!
This city is all right, but there's no place like Ithaca!
Penelope will have to wait a long time before she sees me again! This city is so great!
I've seen worse troubles in my travels!
Visualize defeating the monster. Okay, I'm ready to go!
This city's hygiene is terrible! I'm glad I'm impervious to illness!
Maybe the Graeae had it right. What's the use of having a mouthful of teeth when there's no food?
If Medusa came around, there'd be a lot of statues of people sitting around doing nothing. This city needs more jobs!
I answer to no man, vassal or no vassal!
A rebellion in progress! I hope you put the brats down!
Considering it was built by ordinary mortals, I guess this city isn't too bad.
It is truly a pleasure to live in this city!
Wow! Did you draw that here all by yourself?
Once I put on my winged sandals, I'll be ready for any monster that comes this way!
Hygiene here is poor. With the help of a healer and some food they might yet recover.
I can't believe this city would let a hero go hungry!
There really aren't enough jobs here. This never happened when I ran Athens!
Theseus living in a vassal city? Unthinkable!
This rebellion will do no one good. Someone should put an end to it.
I'll live here if I have to, but nothing compares to my Athens!
You have matched me as a ruler! Your city is superb!
A good leader would never provoke trouble!
I am the match of any monster!
Smash! Smash! Smash! Heh heh heh heh.
Try to match my strength, fool!
grgrrrrrrrrrr . . . Our stinging bites are our gifts to you!
Worthless clod! I'll tear you apart! Limb from limb
I'll turn this city into a giant sculpture garden! Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
I'll trample anyone in my path!
Feel the pain as I rain blows on your brain!

At the university, young Atlanteans pursue their dreams of becoming curators or astronomers.
With a full faculty of professors, this University can instruct a full class of students.
This University is missing a few professors. Classes are still offered, but students aren't graduating as quickly as they could.
The faculty here has only half the professors it needs. Matriculation has slowed.
This University has only a few professors. Students are having difficulty fulfilling the requirements for their degrees.
Only a professor or two teach at this University. It is taking a long time for students to gain knowledge.
This University has no professors. No one is learning anything about astronomy or museums here.
This University is on fire! If a superintendent doesn't extinguish the fire soon, all will be lost!
A curse is placed on this University. The professors have turned into bumbling idiots, and the students cannot learn.
This University is sanctified! Students are grasping concepts quickly and graduating early!
Here, would-be inventors apprentice themselves to experienced masters.
With a full staff of masters, new inventors are quickly refining their techniques.
This Workshop could use a few more workers. New inventors are still being trained, but not as quickly as they could be.
This Workshop has half of the master inventors that it needs. It is taking longer for new inventors to learn the craft.
This Workshop has only a few workers. New inventors aren't learning to create gadgets very quickly at all.
Hardly any master inventors work here. New inventors thirst for knowledge, but sadly there are few people to provide it.
This Workshop has no master inventors. No new inventors are apprenticed here.
The Workshop has gone up in flames! All the new contraptions here will be lost if a superintendent doesn't put out the fire!
This building is cursed! All of the new inventors' experiments are failing!
This Workshop is sanctified! Apprentice inventors are quickly becoming masters!
In the quiet reading rooms of the Bibliotheke, scholars learn about the world around them.
This Bibliotheke is fully staffed. A full complement of scholars is reading up on fascinating topics.
This Bibliotheke is missing a few workers. Scholars are having a little trouble finding the books they need.
With only half the workers it needs, this Bibliotheke is having difficulty servicing local scholars.
Only a few workers are employed here. Only the smallest number of scholars are learning anything new.
Very few workers are employed at this Bibliotheke. The bookshelves are in disarray, and scholars are barely learning anything new.
No one works at this Bibliotheke. The reading room has been closed, and no scholars are learning anything.
This Bibliotheke is on fire! If a superintendent doesn't come soon, all the books here will be lost.
This Bibliotheke is cursed! The pages of the books here have been wiped clean, and scholars have no knowledge to share.
This Bibliotheke is sanctified! Scholars are reading books quicker than ever!
Astronomers watch the skies here at the observatory.
This Observatory has employees, but no astronomers! Make sure astronomers can make their way from a University to the Observatory.
Astronomers are observing the night skies and speculating on the nature of the universe.
This Observatory has no employees! It needs workers to help the astronomers track the movement of heavenly bodies.
Viewing: The Dark Side of the Moon
Viewing: Comets: Bad Omens or Jolly Balls of Ice?
Viewing: Big Asteroids and Fiery Meteors
The Observatory is on fire! Astronomers are running for their lives!
The Observatory is cursed! The Astronomers here are too busy gazing at their belly buttons to look at the sky!
The Observatory is sanctified! Astronomers are discovering new stars and planets at a remarkable rate!
The creature comforts of the Laboratory allow inventors to devise brilliant new gadgets!
This Laboratory has workers, but lacks inventors! Nothing new is being produced here.
The inventors here are busy working on the latest modern convenience.
The Laboratory has no employees. Inventors cannot work unless there is a staff fetching them refreshments!
Nothing new is being invented here.
The Laboratory is on fire! All of the new inventions will go up in smoke if a superintendent doesn't put out the fire!
The Laboratory is cursed! The inventors here keep reinventing the wheel!
The Laboratory is sanctified! Dozens of time-saving inventions are being produced!
The Museum is host to fascinating artifacts from all over the world.
This Museum has employees, but it lacks curators to take care of the collection!
The curators here are busy tending to the collection and spreading word of their latest finds.
This Museum has no employees to take care of day-to-day operations. Curators refuse to work here.
No exhibits are on display here.
Current exhibit on display for another
Exhibit: The Evolution of Atlantean Dress
Exhibit: Early Settlements in the Mediterranean
The Museum is on fire! If a superintendent doesn't put out the fire, all the exhibits will be destroyed!
The Museum is cursed! The curators have no idea what interests the public, and are working on an exhibit of lint!
The Museum is sanctified! More curators than normal are discussing the latest exhibit.
People don't feel well. I hope it's not scurvy!
I'm so hungry! There's not so much as a rind to munch on!
Who will protect us if we're invaded? Our defenses are impaired!
I hate being a vassal! It stinks!
Have you heard that a subject is rebelling against us? That's just nutty!
This city isn't too bad, but it could be much sweeter.
This city is the apple of my eye! You're a great leader!
My what a lovely tree this is! My pruning is giving it the most attractive shape!
I love the harvest! It's great to see the fruits of my labor!
People are sick here, and I don't feel so well myself. But, I'd never miss a day of deer hunting!
Deer are already skittish, and now I have to worry about my growling stomach scaring them off! I need food!
People here are worried about attacks. I say, who cares if we have no defense? Bring it on!
Forget about being a vassal! Let's revolt against our rival!
What an insult! We should pound our rebelling subject into the ground!
If the deer hunting wasn't so good here, I'd move. This city isn't that great.
I'd like to give you a hearty slap on the back and a firm handshake. This city is awesome!
I'm handy with a spear, but not that handy! Aaaaaaaaaaaargh!
Get ready, deer, because here I come!
The ladies love it when I come back from the hunt with fresh meat!
I should really be home in bed, nursing my illness like everyone else. But cows wait for no man.
Are there Harpies around, and am I Phineus? Every scrap of food has been taken from me! I'm starved!
I don't like the looks of things, partner. If we're attacked, we'll surely be defeated by our foe!
There's nothing worse than being a vassal! Even the cows are mad!
I can't believe our subject is rebelling! Someone must have steered them wrong!
If I weren't so loyal to my cows, I'd move along to another city.
I am happy that my home is on this range! There's not a better spot in the world!
It's time to head 'em up and move 'em out! Run!
I love cow-wrangling time! It reminds me that I'm alive!
Until we meet again, little cow, have fun in the pasture!
The poor people in the city are wan and pale from illness. A plague could break out!
I long for the tiniest morsel of food to touch my tongue! I've never been hungrier!
I feel so unsafe! Our city has little hope of fending off any invaders!
The burden of being a vassal chafes! At least I can take solace in my work.
Some foolish city is rebelling against us! They do not know what they are doing!
This city is like so many other cities. Nothing makes it stand out!
This city is the pride of the world! I can't imagine a better place to live!
Even the powers of orichalc can't save me! Help!
I pray to the great gods that the orichalc may be plentiful and easily mined!
This orichalc ore, imbued with beauty and power, is sure to serve its user well!
I'm leaving lickety-split, and you'd better, too!
I'm as excited as Phaethon! I can't wait to take the new chariots for a test drive! Vroom, vroom!
There's nothing more beautiful than a well-made chariot! With the right horse, it can go from 0 to 40 in three minutes!
If, as a wise man said, 'Attention to health is life's greatest hindrance,' then people here are much hindered indeed.
A playwright says, 'Hunger knows no friend but its feeder.' Why not befriend hunger and feed your people?
It's your 'duty to select natures which are fitted for the task of guarding the city.' Yet, our defenses are weak!
How can we submit to another city? We should take the sage's advice and 'make war that we may live in peace!'
Our subject has 'become a rebel to law and convention instead of the conformer that he was.' Let's put a stop to that!
If 'Those who are too smart to engage in politics are punished by being governed by those who are dumber,' I've only myself to blame!
A bard says, 'We would often be sorry if our wishes were gratified.' This city fulfills my wishes, and I am not sorry!
It's said that 'those whom the gods love die young.' I must be well-loved, because I think I'll soon be dead! Aaaargh!
Atlas' beautiful daughters, the Pleiades, are crying in the heavens! They're distraught that so many are ill!
Why won't some god turn me into a constellation so I won't be hungry any more?
Our defenses are weak! Are you expecting Orion to shoot arrows from the sky to protect us?
By Selene, how could you let another city eclipse our strength! We should rebel against our rival!
A subject is rebelling? That defies the rules of the cosmos!
I've heard that the stars shine brighter at other cities, but I suppose this city is okay.
I've thanked the gods billions and billions of times for this city and its fine ruler!
What is that that I see just over the horizon? Oh, no!
So many people are sick! I hope I don't fall ill -- the science world would be lost without me!
Hunger... is... making... me... weak... so... very... weak!
I don't think the city's defenses are calibrated correctly. An enemy would easily defeat us if we were attacked!
I would rather never invent another thing than be a vassal!
One of our subjects thinks it is better off without us. It's an interesting hypothesis, but it's false!
Until my latest invention, InstaKing500, is finished, I guess I'll put up with you. You could make this city so much better!
This city is great! What's your formula for success?
I must run and save my precious inventions! Aaaaaargh!
If I were creating a 'Sick People' exhibit, I'd have plenty of specimens to choose from! People are quite ill!
I can't think about museum work at a time like this! I'm starved!
Our weak defenses are very inviting to our enemies! I hope no one accepts the invitation!
Our forefathers would be ashamed! How can we be some other city's vassal?
Our rebellious subject can't reasonably expect to break free from us. It defies logic!
I would place this city is the mediocre category.
This city is in a class all by itself. It is the finest ever!
My collection is big enough, thank you. Aaaaaaaargh!
My true aim will make no difference against an immortal! Run!
If we aren't going to be attacked, I'd much rather be home!
Ooooh! The enemy will be here soon! I can't wait!
Foolish enemy! Did you think that women cannot show courage?
May the gods guide my arrows! Our enemy is strong, and I need all the help I can get!
Good god! No wait, bad god! Help!
My spear is at the ready, but there is no enemy! Maybe there's some other spear carrier who could use this spear.
The moment I've been waiting for will be here soon! I cannot wait to try out this spear in the heat of battle!
I could fight this enemy with one hand tied behind my back!
Now I remember why Atlanteans are peaceful people! This enemy is too tough!
While I'm sure that I cut a striking figure out here in my chariot, you ought to send me home if there's no fighting.
I wish that the enemy would get here already! I'm tired of waiting!
Look how hard our puny enemy is trying to defeat us. How cute!
If I don't make it back alive, tell my mother she can have my best amphora.
Yikes! I have a feeling this tower isn't going to be enough to protect me!
I've scanned the horizon in all directions, and there are no enemies in site!
Enemies are on their way! I'll have a warm welcome - a downright hot welcome - ready for them!
This is like spearing fish in a barrel! This enemy presents no challenge at all!
It will take all of our strength to turn back this formidable foe!
Become more plentiful under my guiding hand!
What have you done to deserve this? If you don't know, I'm not going to tell you!
At my command, the goods in the Agoras will multiply!
Out of this city, Zeus! And don't come back, if you know what's good for you!
Nothing is more beautiful to me than a large pile of construction materials!
Don't bother begging for mercy, mortal! Feel the chill of my cold heart!
May the materials here last longer than expected, and may this building stretch toward the sky!
Mummy Echidna and Daddy Typhon will be proud of their little girl when I destroy this city!
I am the mother of all monsters! You've met your match.
I'll snatch you up and eat you, I will!
What walks on four legs in the morning, two legs at noon, three legs at dusk and screams in abject terror now?
Too bad your people don't have a hero's strength. They'd be a lot less sickly.
People complain that they're hungry. As for me, I laugh in the face of hunger. Ha ha!
I'm glad to see you've placed so much faith in me, but I'm going to need help fending off enemies if anyone attacks.
Living in a city that is subject to another is an affront to my honor. You should rebel.
You need to reign in that rebelling subject of yours.
If I had my druthers, I wouldn't live here. But, duty calls.
You have worked wonders with this city! You are the Bellerophon of leaders!
I'll fight anything, whether it be mortal, monster or god!
Monster-fighting is my favorite pastime! I can't wait for the creature to get here!
What's wrong with people here? They miss a few meals or some visits from a healer, and they fall ill, just like that!
I can't even see people's ribs! How can they complain about being hungry?
I like your moxie. Facing the world with weak defenses, just inviting an enemy to attack!
How could you let a rival outmatch you? You're not as capable as I thought!
Are you just going to sit there while one of your subjects rebels against you? Where's your pride?
I'd rather be sailing the seas on the Argo than living in this city!
This city is as wonderful as a golden apple!
I bet I've fought monsters bigger and fiercer than the one on the way.
